title: Act on Road Traffic and on Amendments to Certain Acts (Road Traffic Act)

parts.Změna zákona o Policii České republiky: Amendment to the Act on the Police of the Czech Republic
parts.Změna zákona o bezpečnosti a plynulosti: Amendment to the Act on the Safety and Smoothness of Road Traffic
parts.Změna zákona o přestupcích: Amendment to the Act on Misdemeanors
parts.Změna zákona o silniční dopravě: Amendment to the Road Transport Act

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text: This Act regulates the rights and obligations of road users on roads pursuant to a special legal regulation^1) (hereinafter referred to as „roads“), road traffic rules, the regulation and management of road traffic, driving licences and driving permits, and defines the scope of authority and powers of state administration bodies and the Police of the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as „the police“) in matters of road traffic.

p2.heading: Definition of basic terms

p3.heading: Basic conditions for participation in road traffic
p3.text:
(1) A person who, due to age or reduced physical or mental capabilities, could endanger the safety of road traffic, shall not participate in road traffic. This shall not apply if the person themselves or another person has taken such measures to prevent the endangerment of road traffic safety.

(2) Only a person who is sufficiently physically and mentally capable of driving a vehicle or riding an animal, and who possesses the necessary command of vehicle operation or animal riding and the regulations on road traffic, may drive a vehicle or ride an animal.

(3) Only a person who holds the relevant driving licence pursuant to § 81, or an applicant for a driving licence who is undergoing instruction as part of motor vehicle driving training or taking a motor vehicle driving test under the supervision of a driving instructor, may drive a motor vehicle.

(4) A road user must not use technical means and devices that prevent or affect the function of technical means used by the Police or Military Police in supervising road traffic safety.

p4.heading: Duties of a road user

p5.heading: Duties of the driver
p5.text:
(1) In addition to the duties specified in § 4, the driver is further obliged to:

- a) use a vehicle that meets the technical conditions stipulated by a special legal regulation,^2)

- b) fully concentrate on driving the vehicle or riding the animal and monitor the situation in road traffic,

- c) adapt the driving to the technical characteristics of the vehicle or the physical characteristics of the animal,

- d) exercise increased caution, especially towards children, persons with limited mobility and orientation,^6) severely disabled persons, and animals, and to consider a vehicle transporting children, a novice driver, or a severely disabled person marked according to the implementing legal regulation, and a training vehicle marked according to a special legal regulation,^4)

- e) rectify a defect on the spot if, during the journey, they discover that the vehicle or load does not meet the stipulated conditions specified in § 52; if they cannot do so, they may continue driving at an appropriate speed only to the nearest place where the defect can be rectified; in doing so, they must take such measures to ensure that road traffic safety is not endangered and no damage to the road or the environment occurs during the journey,

- f) submit to a breath test at the request of a police officer and, in case of a positive finding, also to a medical examination with blood or urine sampling to ascertain whether they are under the influence of alcohol,

- g) submit to a specialized medical examination at the request of a police officer to ascertain whether they are under the influence of an addictive substance according to a special legal regulation,^7)

- h) with the exception of a tram driver, allow a pedestrian who is on a pedestrian crossing or clearly intends to use it, to cross the roadway undisturbed and safely; therefore, the driver of such a vehicle must approach the pedestrian crossing at such a speed that they can stop the vehicle before the pedestrian crossing, and if necessary, they are obliged to stop the vehicle before the pedestrian crossing,

- i) ensure the safety of the transported person or animal^8) and the safe transport of the load,

- j) secure the transported animal so that it does not endanger the driver and transported persons,

- k) ensure that the necessary number of capable and properly instructed persons are engaged for the journey if road traffic safety requires it.

(2) The driver must not:

- a) consume an alcoholic beverage or use an addictive substance during the journey,

- b) drive a vehicle or ride an animal immediately after consuming an alcoholic beverage or using an addictive substance, or within such a period after consuming an alcoholic beverage or using an addictive substance when they could still be under their influence,

- c) drive a vehicle or ride an animal if their ability to drive a vehicle or ride an animal is reduced due to their health condition,

- d) hand over the driving of a vehicle or entrust an animal to a person who does not meet the conditions pursuant to § 3 para. 2 and 3 or who is under the influence of alcohol or another addictive substance or whose ability to drive a vehicle or ride an animal is reduced due to their health condition,

- e) throw objects from the vehicle,

- f) endanger pedestrians crossing the road onto which the driver is turning, when turning onto a place outside the road, when entering the road, and when turning around or reversing.

p6.heading: Duties of a motor vehicle driver
p6.text:
(1) In addition to the duties specified in § 4 and 5, the driver of a motor vehicle shall further be obliged to:

- a) fasten the safety belt while driving in a seat that is mandatorily equipped with a safety belt according to a special legal regulation;^2)

- b) transport a person under 12 years of age or a person under 18 years of age and shorter than 150 cm in the seat next to the driver only with the use of a child restraint system;^2) a place in the sidecar of a motorcycle is not considered a seat next to the driver;

- c) transport a person under 18 years of age and shorter than 150 cm in a seat other than that specified in letter b), which is equipped with a safety belt, only with the use of a child restraint system; this does not apply to persons for whom their health condition or physical disposition does not allow the use of a child restraint system;

- d) secure the child restraint system in the manner prescribed by a special legal regulation;^2)

- e) prevent the use of a child restraint system in seats in front of and next to which an airbag is located, if this device cannot be deactivated for the period of use of the child restraint system;

- f) wear a properly fitted and fastened protective helmet of an approved type according to a special legal regulation^2) on their head while riding a motorcycle or moped, and protect their eyes in an appropriate manner while riding, for example with glasses or a visor, unless this reduces driving safety, for example in rain or snow.

(2) The provision of paragraph 1 letter a) does not apply to drivers when reversing a vehicle, driving instructors during training, and persons who cannot do so for health reasons based on a medical certificate. The medical certificate must be carried with them while driving; in cases where it is not a permanent condition, the validity of the medical certificate must be time-limited to a maximum of one year. The particulars of the medical certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(3) The driver of a motor vehicle must carry with them while driving:

- a) a driving license;

- b) a vehicle registration certificate according to a special legal regulation;^2)

- c) a document proving liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle according to a special legal regulation.^9)

(4) Upon request of a police officer, the driver of a motor vehicle is obliged to present the documents according to paragraph 3 to the police officer for inspection.

(5) The driver of a motor vehicle is obliged, upon request of a police officer, to submit the vehicle to an inspection of the maximum permissible axle load, the maximum permissible weight of the vehicle or combination of vehicles, or the technical condition of the vehicle or combination of vehicles.

(6) The driver of a motor vehicle is obliged, upon entering or exiting the territory of the Czech Republic, upon request of a customs authority officer, to submit the vehicle to an inspection of the maximum permissible axle load, the maximum permissible weight of the vehicle or combination of vehicles.

(7) The driver of an armed forces vehicle is obliged, upon request of a police officer or military police officer, to submit this vehicle or combination of vehicles to an inspection of its technical condition.

p8.heading: Duties of a tram driver
p8.text:
(1) For tram drivers, § 4, 5 and § 7 para. 1 letters a) and c) apply.

(2) The tram driver is obliged to carry an identity card and a qualification certificate for driving a rail vehicle according to a special legal regulation^11) while driving the tram. Upon request of a police officer, the tram driver is obliged to present these documents to the police officer for inspection.

p9.heading: Duties of a transported person
p9.text:
(1) A transported person is obliged to:

- a) fasten the safety belt while driving in a seat, if the seat is equipped with one;

- b) use a protective helmet of an approved type according to a special legal regulation^2) while riding a motorcycle or moped, which is worn and properly fastened on their head;

- c) not endanger the safety of road traffic by their behavior, in particular not to restrict the driver in the safe control of the vehicle;

- d) follow the driver's instructions, especially when getting into and out of the vehicle.

(2) The duties according to paragraph 1 letter a) do not apply to a person over 18 years of age and shorter than 150 cm, and a person who cannot do so for health reasons based on a medical certificate. The medical certificate must be carried with them while driving; in cases where it is not a permanent condition, the validity of the medical certificate must be time-limited to a maximum of one year. The particulars of the medical certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation. If a physically disabled person is transported in a vehicle in a wheelchair, the wheelchair and the physically disabled person must be secured by a special restraint system of an approved design.^2)

(3) A transported person must not throw objects out of the vehicle.

(4) A transported person riding a motorcycle must have their feet on the footrests and must not smoke.

(5) Side seating on a motorcycle is prohibited.

Part 2

Vehicle operator

p10.heading: Duties of the vehicle operator
p10.text:
(1) The vehicle operator must not

- a) order or permit the use of a vehicle in road traffic that does not meet the conditions stipulated by a special legal regulation,^2)

- b) entrust the driving of a vehicle to a person who does not meet the conditions of § 3 para 2,

- c) entrust the driving of a motor vehicle to a person who does not meet the conditions under § 3 para 3.

(2) The vehicle operator is obliged to ensure that the color scheme and marking of the vehicle are not interchangeable with the special color scheme of Military Police vehicles according to a special legal regulation,^12) police vehicles according to a special legal regulation^13) and customs administration vehicles according to a special legal regulation.^14)

Part 3

Rules of Road Traffic

Section 1

Driving Vehicles

p11.heading: Direction and Manner of Driving
p11.text:
(1) On a road, traffic proceeds on the right, and unless special circumstances prevent it, along the right edge of the roadway, unless otherwise stipulated.

(2) A motor vehicle driver may enter the shoulder only when stopping or parking or, if necessary, when overtaking, avoiding, turning off, or turning around; in doing so, increased caution must be exercised.

p12.heading: Driving in Lanes
p12.text:
(1) Outside a municipality, on a road with two or more lanes marked on the roadway in one direction of travel, traffic proceeds in the right lane. Other lanes may be used if necessary for overtaking, passing, turning around, or turning off.

(2) Within a municipality, on a road with two or more lanes marked on the roadway in one direction of travel, a motor vehicle driver may use any lane for driving; in doing so, it is not considered overtaking if vehicles in one lane travel faster than vehicles in another lane. If vehicles traveling simultaneously in all lanes would impede a faster-moving vehicle, the driver in the leftmost lane must vacate this lane as soon as possible; this does not apply if the driver uses the leftmost lane for turning off, turning around, or in parallel driving according to paragraph 3. A driver of a truck with a total weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a vehicle combination whose total length exceeds 7 m, a special motor vehicle, and a motorcycle with a maximum permissible speed of up to 45 km.h^-1 may use the leftmost lane for driving only if it is necessary for overtaking, passing, turning around, or turning off.

(3) If, on a road with two or more lanes in one direction of travel, the traffic density is such that continuous streams of vehicles are formed, in which a motor vehicle driver can only travel at a speed dependent on the speed of vehicles traveling in front of them, motor vehicles may travel in parallel (hereinafter referred to as „parallel driving“); in doing so, it is not considered overtaking if vehicles in one of the lanes travel faster than vehicles in another lane.

(4) On a road with three or more lanes marked on the roadway in one direction of travel, a driver of a truck with a total weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a vehicle combination whose total length exceeds 7 m, a special motor vehicle, and a motorcycle with a maximum permissible speed of up to 45 km.h^-1 may use exclusively the two lanes closest to the right edge of the roadway for driving; other lanes may be used if necessary for overtaking, turning around, or turning off.

(5) A driver may change from one lane to another only if they do not endanger or impede a driver traveling in the lane into which they are changing; in doing so, they must signal a change of direction. In parallel driving, drivers of vehicles traveling in a through lane shall allow drivers of vehicles merging into that lane from a lane that has ceased to be a through lane to enter so that vehicles traveling in the through lane and vehicles merging into it can alternate one by one into the traffic stream of the through lane. Where two lanes merge into one without it being clear which of them is the through lane, the driver traveling in the left lane must not endanger the driver traveling in the right lane.

(6) Before entering a through lane, the driver must use an acceleration lane; where there is no acceleration lane, the driver must give way to vehicles traveling in the through lane.

(7) If lanes are not marked on the roadway, for the purposes of paragraphs 3 and 5, a lane means a part of the roadway allowing the travel of vehicles other than two-wheeled vehicles (motorcycles) in a single file traffic stream.

Driving in Special Cases

p13.text:
(1) Along a boarding or safety island, traffic proceeds on the right; traffic may proceed on the left only if an obstacle prevents driving on the right or if it is safer considering the dimensions of the vehicle or load. When driving along a boarding or safety island, increased caution must be exercised. Along a tram, traffic proceeds on the right, unless the traffic sign „Overtaking Tram“ permits driving on the left.

(2) A tram track at roadway level may be entered longitudinally only when overtaking, passing, turning off, turning around, entering a road, or if special circumstances require it, for example, if there is not enough space between the tram track and the edge of the roadway; a tram track raised above or lowered below the roadway level or otherwise separated from the roadway, for example by a curb, may only be crossed transversely, and only at a place adapted for this purpose. When entering a tram track, the driver must not endanger or impede the tram.

p14.text:
(1) If a traffic lane is marked with the traffic sign "Reserved Lane" (hereinafter referred to as "reserved lane") for a specific type of vehicle, § 13 (2) shall apply mutatis mutandis to drivers of other vehicles, and if the reserved lane is marked on a tramway strip, § 21 (7) shall also apply.

(2) If the driver of a vehicle for which a lane is reserved moves from the reserved lane into an adjacent lane, the driver of the vehicle travelling in that lane must allow this by reducing speed or, if necessary, by stopping the vehicle. The driver of a vehicle in a reserved lane is obliged to signal a change of direction and must not endanger drivers of other vehicles.

(3) If a vehicle for which a lane is reserved travels in the reserved lane or a tram travels at a different speed than other vehicles travelling in the same direction, this is not mutual overtaking.

(4) If traffic in a reserved lane marked on a tramway strip is controlled by light signals for trams, the driver of a vehicle travelling in this lane shall be governed by these light signals.

p15.text:
(1) Behind a public transport vehicle that has stopped in a municipality at a stop without an island platform or without a platform on a raised tramway strip, the driver of another vehicle must stop; if there are multiple public transport vehicles at the stop, they must stop behind the second one. They may only continue driving when they no longer endanger passengers who are boarding or alighting. This does not apply if a bus or trolleybus stops at the edge of the roadway.

(2) Behind a bus marked "Bus carrying children", which has stopped at a designated stop, the driver of another vehicle must stop. They may only continue driving after the bus has departed from the stop. Drivers of oncoming vehicles must take into account the possibility of children running into the roadway and adjust their driving so as not to endanger the children.

p16.heading: Circumventing
p16.text: A driver who, when circumventing a vehicle that has stopped or is standing, or when circumventing an obstruction to traffic on a road, or a pedestrian, deviates from their direction of travel, must not endanger or restrict oncoming drivers and must not endanger other road users. They must signal a change of direction.

p17.heading: Overtaking
p17.text:
(1) Overtaking is done on the left. A vehicle changing direction to the left may be overtaken on the right if there is no longer any doubt about its further direction of travel. When driving in an acceleration lane or a deceleration lane, a vehicle travelling in the through lane may also be overtaken on the right. A deceleration lane is an auxiliary lane intended for vehicles to turn off (exit) from a through lane.

(2) A driver who, when overtaking, deviates from their direction of travel, must signal a change of direction and must not endanger or restrict drivers travelling behind them.

(3) A driver who, after overtaking, merges in front of the vehicle they have overtaken, must signal a change of direction and must not endanger or restrict the driver of the vehicle they have overtaken.

(4) The driver of an overtaken vehicle must not increase speed or otherwise impede overtaking.

(5) A driver must not overtake

- a) if they do not have a clear view ahead for a distance necessary for safe overtaking,

- b) if they cannot safely merge in front of the vehicle or vehicles they intend to overtake,

- c) if they would endanger or restrict oncoming drivers or endanger other road users,

- d) on a pedestrian crossing and immediately before it,

- e) if the driver of the vehicle ahead is signalling a change of direction to the left and overtaking on the right according to paragraph 1 or overtaking in another free lane marked on the roadway in the same direction of travel is not possible,

- f) at an intersection and in close proximity before it; this prohibition does not apply to a driver travelling on a main road, or if it is overtaking on the right according to paragraph 1; at an intersection with controlled traffic, overtaking is permitted only with a light signal or instruction "Proceed",

- g) at a railway crossing and in close proximity before it.

p18.heading: Driving speed
p18.text:
(1) The driver must adjust the driving speed primarily to their abilities, the characteristics of the vehicle and its load, the anticipated construction and traffic-technical condition of the road, its category and class, weather conditions, and other foreseeable circumstances; they may only drive at a speed that allows them to stop the vehicle within the distance for which they have a clear view.

(2) The driver must not

- a) suddenly reduce speed or suddenly stop, unless required by road safety,

- b) impede the smooth flow of traffic on roads, especially by unnecessarily slow driving and slow overtaking.

(3) The driver of a motor vehicle with a maximum permissible mass not exceeding 3,500 kg and a bus may drive outside a municipality at a speed of no more than 90 km.h^-1; on a motorway and a road for motor vehicles at a speed of no more than 130 km.h^-1. The driver of another motor vehicle may drive at a speed of no more than 80 km.h^-1.

(4) In a municipality, the driver may drive at a speed of no more than 50 km.h^-1, and if it is a motorway or a road for motor vehicles, no more than 80 km.h^-1.

(5) The driver must not exceed the maximum permitted speed of the vehicle,^2) and if it is a vehicle combination, the maximum permitted speed of any of the vehicles in the combination.

(6) The maximum permitted speed according to paragraphs 3 and 4 may be reduced by local traffic regulation on roads according to § 61 (2).

(7) The maximum permitted speed according to paragraph 4 may be increased by local traffic regulation on roads according to § 61 (2), but by a maximum of 30 km.h^-1.

(8) When using snow chains on a vehicle, the driver may drive at a speed of no more than 50 km.h^-1.

(9) The provisions of paragraphs 3, 4 and 8 do not apply to drivers of intelligence services^15) and designated police units, if it is absolutely necessary for the performance of tasks stipulated by a special legal regulation,^16) however, they are obliged to exercise due caution so as not to endanger road traffic safety on roads. Police units shall be determined by the Minister of the Interior.

p19.heading: Distance between vehicles
p19.text:
(1) The driver of a vehicle travelling behind another vehicle must maintain a sufficient safe distance from it to be able to avoid a collision in the event of a sudden reduction in speed or sudden stop of the vehicle in front.

(2) The driver of a motor vehicle with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg, a vehicle combination whose total length exceeds 10 m, and a special vehicle^2) must, outside of a municipality, maintain such a distance behind the vehicle travelling in front that an overtaking vehicle can safely merge in front of it; this does not apply when preparing to overtake, during overtaking, and during parallel driving.

(3) A driver who does not intend or is unable to pass a tram on the right must drive behind it at such a distance as to allow other drivers to pass the tram.

p20.heading: Avoiding
p20.text: Drivers of oncoming vehicles avoid each other to the right, in good time and sufficiently. If they cannot avoid each other safely, the driver on whose side of the road there is an obstacle or narrowed roadway must give way. If one of them has to reverse, the one for whom it is easier or less dangerous shall do so. If it is not possible to avoid an oncoming tram on the right, it shall be avoided on the left.

p21.heading: Turning
p21.text:
(1) When turning at an intersection or into a place located off the road, the driver must signal a change of direction; when turning, the driver must not endanger drivers travelling behind and must exercise increased caution.

(2) If circumstances require it, for example when transporting a long load, the driver must ensure a safe turn with the assistance of a competent and properly instructed person.

(3) Before turning right, the driver must position the vehicle as close as possible to the right edge of the roadway; if, due to the dimensions of the vehicle or load, the driver must deviate to the left from the direction of travel, the driver shall always only signal a change of direction to the right. Before turning left, the driver must position the vehicle as far left as possible in the part of the roadway designated for their direction of travel, taking into account the dimensions of the vehicle or load and the width of the roadway. If drivers of oncoming vehicles turn left, they shall avoid each other to the left.

(4) A driver who is leaving a through lane when turning must enter the turning lane as soon as possible, if one is marked.

(5) A driver turning left must give way to oncoming motor and non-motor vehicles, riders on animals, oncoming organized groups of pedestrians and animal herders with animals, trams travelling in both directions, and vehicles travelling in a designated lane for which that lane is reserved.

(6) A driver turning right must give way to vehicles travelling in a designated lane for which that lane is reserved. Where driving alongside a tram on the left is permitted, the driver must also give way to the tram.

(7) A tram that, when turning or changing direction, crosses the path of a vehicle travelling on its right or left side and signals a change of direction, has priority.

p22.heading: Driving through an intersection
p22.text:
(1) A driver approaching an intersection on a minor road marked with the traffic sign „Give Way!“ or „Stop, Give Way!“ must give way to vehicles or riders on animals approaching on the main road or to an organized group of pedestrians or animal herders with animals approaching on the main road.

(2) If priority is not established by the provisions of paragraph 1, the driver must give way to vehicles or riders on animals approaching from the right or to an organized group of pedestrians or animal herders with animals approaching from the right.

(3) A driver must not enter an intersection if the situation does not allow them to continue driving in and beyond the intersection, thus forcing them to stop the vehicle in the intersection.

(4) At the command of the traffic sign „Stop, Give Way!“, the driver must stop the vehicle at a place from which they have a proper view into the intersection.

(5) A driver entering a roundabout must give way to vehicles already on the roundabout.

p23.heading: Entering a road
p23.text:
(1) When entering a road from a place located off the road, the driver must give way to vehicles or riders on animals travelling on the road or to an organized group of pedestrians or animal herders with animals walking on the road. This also applies when entering another road from a cycle path or from a residential or pedestrian zone.

(2) If circumstances require it, especially due to insufficient visibility, the driver must ensure safe entry onto the road with the assistance of a competent and properly instructed person.

(3) Vehicles entering the road must be cleaned beforehand so as not to pollute the road.

p24.heading: Turning around and reversing
p24.text:
(1) When turning around, the provisions on turning (§ 21) apply mutatis mutandis, and when turning around at an intersection, the provisions on driving through an intersection (§ 22) also apply.

(2) When reversing, the driver must not endanger other road users.

(3) If circumstances require it, especially due to insufficient visibility, the driver must ensure safe turning around or reversing with the assistance of a competent and properly instructed person.

(4) The driver must not turn around or reverse

- a) in places with poor visibility or otherwise dangerous places, for example, in and in the immediate vicinity of a blind bend, before, on, and after a blind crest of an uphill road,

- b) at an intersection with controlled traffic and in its immediate vicinity,

- c) at a pedestrian crossing,

- d) at a cycle crossing,

- e) at a railway crossing and in its immediate vicinity,

- f) in a tunnel and in its immediate vicinity,

- g) on a one-way road; however, the driver may reverse if it is absolutely necessary, for example, to enter or exit a line of parked vehicles.

Stopping and standing

p25.text:
(1) A driver may stop and park only on the right in the direction of travel, as close as possible to the edge of the road, in a single line and parallel to the edge of the road.

(2) On a one-way road, a driver may stop and park on both the right and left.

(3) When parking, at least one lane at least 3 m wide must remain clear for each direction of travel; when stopping, at least one lane at least 3 m wide must remain clear for both directions of travel.

(4) When stopping and parking, the driver must not prevent other drivers from pulling out of a line of parked vehicles. When stopping and parking next to a vehicle marked with the “Marking of a vehicle transporting a severely mobility-impaired person”, a lateral distance of at least 1.2 m must be maintained.

(5) If a driver pulls over to the edge of the road or to the pavement for the purpose of stopping or parking, they must signal a change of direction.

(6) A driver of a vehicle that has stopped or parked and is pulling out again from the edge of the road or from the pavement must signal a change of direction and must not endanger other road users. In a municipality, drivers of other vehicles must allow a public transport bus or trolleybus driver to pull out of a stop or a stop lane by reducing their speed, or even by stopping the vehicle; the bus or trolleybus driver must not, however, endanger especially drivers of vehicles travelling in the same direction.

p26.text:
(1) Opening vehicle doors or side panels, as well as getting into or out of a vehicle, is permitted only if the safety of persons getting in or out or other road users is not thereby endangered.

(2) A driver who intends to leave the vehicle in such a way that they cannot intervene immediately if necessary must take measures to ensure that the vehicle cannot endanger road safety and cannot be used unlawfully by another person. If the vehicle is mandatorily equipped with an anti-theft device^2), the driver must use it. Drivers of motor vehicles or vehicle combinations mandatorily equipped with wheel chocks^2) must use them if it is necessary to secure the vehicle or combination against movement.

(3) A driver of a motor vehicle that is mandatorily equipped with a portable warning triangle^2) must use this triangle during an emergency stop, for example, when travel is interrupted due to a vehicle or cargo defect, as a result of a traffic accident, or due to sudden illness, if such a vehicle constitutes an obstruction to road traffic. The triangle must be placed on the edge of the roadway so that it is visible in time and clearly to approaching drivers, at a distance of at least 50 m, on a motorway at least 100 m behind the vehicle. In a municipality, this distance may be shorter if circumstances require it. If the motor vehicle is equipped with a warning light device^2), the driver must use it for at least the period until the warning triangle is placed on the roadway.

p27.heading: Railway Crossing
p27.text:
(1) A driver must not stop and park

- a) on a blind bend and in its immediate vicinity,

- b) before, on, and after a blind summit of a road,

- c) on a pedestrian crossing and at a distance shorter than 5 m before it,

- d) at an intersection and at a distance shorter than 5 m before the intersection boundary and 5 m after it; this prohibition does not apply in a municipality at a “T” shaped intersection on the opposite side of the intersecting road,

- e) in an acceleration or deceleration lane,

- f) at a tram, bus, or trolleybus stop without a boarding island in the section that begins with the traffic sign “Bus Stop”, “Tram Stop”, or “Trolleybus Stop” and ends at a distance of 5 m after the stop sign, and where there is no such traffic sign, at a distance shorter than 30 m before and 5 m after the stop sign; if the stop area is marked by the horizontal traffic sign “Bus or Trolleybus Stop” or “Tram Stop”, this prohibition applies only to the marked area,

- g) at a railway crossing, in an underpass and in a tunnel and at a distance shorter than 15 m before and after them,

- h) in a place where the vehicle would obscure a vertical traffic sign or the horizontal traffic sign “Directional Arrows” or “Text on Roadway”,

- i) in a designated lane,

- j) in lanes marked on the roadway other than the rightmost lane,

- k) at a distance shorter than 5 m from the beginning and end of the horizontal traffic sign “Continuous Longitudinal Line” or a boarding island where at least one lane at least 3 m wide would not remain clear between this traffic sign or boarding island and the vehicle,

- l) on a bridge,

- m) in a tunnel; this does not apply in the case of an emergency stop in a place marked by the traffic sign “Emergency Stop”; in the case of an emergency stop, the driver must switch off the engine,

- n) before entering a road from a field or forest road or from a place located outside the road,

- o) in a reserved parking space, unless it is a vehicle for which the parking space is reserved,

- p) on a tram track,

- r) in other places where road safety, especially the movement of other vehicles, would be endangered.

(2) Between 5:00 AM and 7:00 PM, parking is prohibited where a clear lane at least 3.5 m wide would not remain between the vehicle and the nearest tram rail.

(3) On Class I roads and, in reduced visibility, also on Class II and III roads^1), outside a municipality, stopping and parking is prohibited elsewhere than in places marked by a traffic sign as a parking lot.

(4) On a traffic circle marked with the traffic sign “Circle” or “Change of Circle Direction”, parking is prohibited.

p28.text:
(1) Before a railway crossing, the driver must proceed with particular caution, especially to ascertain whether they can safely cross the railway crossing.

(2) Vehicles before a railway crossing line up one behind the other in the order in which they arrived. Unless it is parallel driving or driving according to § 12 para. 2, vehicles may cross a railway crossing only in a single traffic stream.

(3) At a distance of 50 m before a railway crossing and when crossing it, the driver may drive at a maximum speed of 30 km.h^-1. If the intermittent white light of the railway crossing safety device signal is illuminated, the driver may drive at a maximum speed of 50 km.h^-1 50 m before the railway crossing and when crossing it. When crossing a railway crossing, the driver must not unnecessarily prolong the time of crossing it.

(4) If a vehicle stops on a railway crossing, its driver must remove the vehicle from the railway track,^17) and if unable to do so, must immediately do everything to ensure that drivers of rail vehicles are warned of the danger in time.

(5) Before a railway crossing where the traffic sign „Stůj, dej přednost v jízdě!“ [Stop, give way!] is placed, the driver must stop the vehicle at such a place from which they have an adequate view of the track.

p30.heading: Signal of change of direction
p30.text:
(1) The driver must give a signal of change of direction, in addition to the cases specified in individual provisions, also always when changing direction, deviating from it, or if required by road traffic safety.

(2) The driver must give a signal of change of direction in time before commencing the driving maneuver, taking into account the circumstances of road traffic, especially drivers behind them and the nature of the driving maneuver.

(3) A signal of change of direction is given by turn signals. If the vehicle is not equipped with them or in case of their malfunction, the signal is given by extending the arm. An arm bent at the elbow upwards signals a change of direction to the opposite side. If circumstances require, especially if the signal given by turn signals or arm is not sufficiently visible, for example due to the width of the load or in reduced visibility, the signal must be given in another clear manner, for example by a competent and duly instructed person. Otherwise, the driver may perform the intended driving maneuver only when and in such a way that other road users are neither endangered nor restricted.

(4) The driver shall keep the signal of change of direction given by turn signals on only until the completion of the change of direction, deviation from it, or until the vehicle occupies its place in the lane into which it is moving. The signal of change of direction given by arm is given only before commencing the driving maneuver.

p31.heading: Warning signals
p31.text:
(1) If it is necessary to avert an imminent danger, the driver gives an audible warning signal. Outside a municipality, the driver may also give an audible warning signal if it is necessary to alert the driver of an overtaken vehicle.

(2) Instead of an audible warning signal, the driver may give a visual warning signal by briefly flashing the high beam or by switching between dipped and high beams; to alert the driver of an overtaken vehicle, they may also give it within a municipality.

(3) If it is necessary to alert other road users to an imminent danger, especially in cases where it is necessary to suddenly reduce driving speed or stop the vehicle, the driver gives a visual warning signal by activating the hazard warning lights.

(4) The warning signal is used only for the time strictly necessary.

Vehicle lighting

p32.text:
(1) When driving in reduced visibility, a vehicle must have its side lights and dipped-beam or main-beam headlights on, if it is equipped with them according to a special legal regulation.^2)

(2) A motorcycle and a moped must have their side lights on when driving, if they are equipped with them according to a special legal regulation,^2) and dipped-beam headlights.

(3) During the period outside the part of the calendar year for which summer time is set according to a special legal regulation,^18) a vehicle must have its side lights and dipped-beam headlights on when driving, or its side lights and daytime running lights, if it is equipped with them according to a special legal regulation.^2)

(4) The driver must not use main-beam headlights if the road is sufficiently and continuously lit, or if the driver of an oncoming vehicle, the driver of a vehicle travelling in front of them, or another road user, train driver, driver of another rail vehicle, or vessel driver could be dazzled. When stopping a vehicle before a railway crossing, the driver must not even use dipped-beam headlights if they could dazzle the driver of a vehicle in the opposite direction.

(5) The driver may only use front fog lights in fog, snowfall, or heavy rain. The driver must always use rear fog lights in fog, snowfall, or heavy rain.

(6) The active surface of the lights must not be covered or excessively dirty.

p33.text:
(1) A motor vehicle or a vehicle combination whose dimensions or the dimensions of its load exceed the limits set by a special legal regulation^2) must have its side lights and dipped-beam headlights on when driving.

(2) A vehicle standing in reduced visibility in a municipality in a place where it obstructs road traffic, or on a road outside a municipality, must have at least its side lights or parking lights on the side facing the centre of the road, or it must be illuminated on the side facing the centre of the road by at least one white non-dazzling light visible from the front and rear. This does not apply in a car park.

p34.heading: Towing of motor vehicles
p34.text:
(1) When towing a motor vehicle, the speed must not exceed 60 km.h^-1.

(2) A motor vehicle may only be towed by rope if it has faultless steering and effective brakes.

(3) A motor vehicle may only be towed by a tow bar if it has faultless steering. If the towed vehicle does not have effective brakes, its instantaneous mass must not be greater than the instantaneous mass of the towing vehicle.

(4) When towing a motor vehicle, the length of the connecting device must be such that the distance between the vehicles is not greater than 6 m; if a rope is used, the distance between the vehicles must not be less than 2.5 m, and if a tow bar is used, it must not be less than 1 m. The connecting device must be clearly marked (tow bar with transverse red and white stripes 75 mm wide, rope with a red flag or sign measuring at least 300 x 300 mm).

(5) The drivers of the towing and towed vehicles are obliged to agree in advance on the method of communication during the journey.

(6) Towing more than one motor vehicle or a motor vehicle with a trailer is prohibited. However, a motor vehicle with a semi-trailer may be towed. Another motor vehicle must not be towed behind a motor vehicle with a trailer. A motorcycle without a sidecar and a moped must not be towed or used as a towing vehicle.

(7) A bus or a motor vehicle towed by means of a special device may only be towed without transported persons.

(8) The towing vehicle must have its side lights and dipped-beam headlights on. The towed vehicle must be visibly marked from behind with a warning triangle, for example behind the glass, on the rear face of the body.

(9) In reduced visibility, the towed vehicle must have its side lights or dipped-beam headlights on. In the event of their failure, the vehicle must be illuminated on the side towards the centre of the road by a non-dazzling white light at the front and at least one red light at the rear; these lights must be clearly visible and must not be placed further than 400 mm from the side outline of the vehicle.

Section 2

Driving vehicles in special cases

Traffic on motorways

p35.text:
(1) Only motor vehicles and vehicle combinations whose maximum permitted speed^2) is not less than 80 km.h^-1 are permitted on a motorway.

(2) The driver may only enter and exit a motorway at designated places.

p36.text:
(1) On a motorway, the driver is prohibited from

- a) stopping and standing anywhere other than in places marked as car parks. In the event of an emergency stop according to § 26 para. 3, the vehicle must stand on the hard shoulder, and only if this is not possible, on the carriageway. Such a vehicle must always be marked by the driver as an obstruction to road traffic; if it is a motor vehicle mandatorily equipped with a portable warning triangle,^2) the driver must place the triangle according to § 26 para. 3,

- b) turning, reversing, and entering the central dividing strip, including places where the strip is interrupted.

(2) If, during the journey, a defect occurs in the vehicle or its load, due to which a speed of at least 80 km.h^-1 cannot be achieved on a flat road, the driver must leave the motorway at the nearest exit.

(3) Towing a motor vehicle is permitted only if it is necessary to remove it from the motorway. The vehicle may only be towed to the nearest exit, where it must leave the motorway.

p37.text: Unless otherwise provided in this section, other provisions of this Act apply to traffic on motorways.

p38.heading: Traffic in residential and pedestrian zones
p38.text: The provisions on traffic on motorways also apply to roads for motor vehicles.

p39.text:
(1) A residential zone is a built-up area, the beginning of which is marked by the traffic sign „Residential Zone“ and the end is marked by the traffic sign „End of Residential Zone“.

(2) A pedestrian zone is an area, the beginning of which is marked by the traffic sign „Pedestrian Zone“ and the end is marked by the traffic sign „End of Pedestrian Zone“.

(3) In a residential and pedestrian zone, pedestrians may use the road over its entire width, and § 53 does not apply to them. Children's games on the road are permitted only in a residential zone.

(4) Entry into a pedestrian zone is permitted only for vehicles indicated in the lower part of the traffic sign according to paragraph 2.

(5) In a residential zone and pedestrian zone, a driver may drive at a maximum speed of 20 km.h^-1. In doing so, they must exercise increased consideration for pedestrians, whom they must not endanger; if necessary, they must stop the vehicle. Parking is permitted only in places designated as parking lots.

(6) For the purpose of organizing traffic, the municipality may, in a generally binding municipal ordinance issued in delegated authority, define local roads or their sections in a residential zone that cannot be used for parking a truck^2) or a road train.^2)

(7) In a residential zone and pedestrian zone, pedestrians must allow vehicles to drive. This also applies to children playing in a residential zone.

p40.heading: Driving of vehicles with priority right of way
p40.text: Unless otherwise provided in this section, other provisions of this Act shall apply to traffic in a residential zone and pedestrian zone.

p41.text:
(1) The driver of a vehicle who, in fulfilling tasks related to the performance of special duties, uses a special blue warning light,^2) possibly supplemented by a special audible warning signal (hereinafter referred to as „vehicle with priority right of way“), is not obliged to comply with § 4 letter c), § 5 para 1 letter f), g) and h), § 6 para 5 and 6, § 7 para 1 letter b), § 11, § 12 para 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6, § 13 to 17, § 18 para 2, 3, 4 and 8, § 19 para 2 and 3, § 20, § 21 para 2, 3 and 4, § 22, 23, § 24 para 3 and 4, § 25 para 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, § 26 para 3, § 27, § 28 para 2, 3 and 5, § 31, § 32 para 6, § 35 para 2, § 36 para 1 and 2 and § 39 para 4 and 5; however, they are obliged to exercise the necessary caution so as not to endanger the safety of traffic on roads.

(2) Vehicles may be equipped with a special audible warning device supplemented by a special blue warning light^2):

- a) of the Ministry of the Interior used by the police and marked according to a special legal regulation,^13)

- b) of the Prison Service of the Czech Republic,

- c) of the military police marked according to a special legal regulation,^12)

- d) of the municipal police,^5) as determined by the municipality,

- e) of fire and rescue services,

- f) of mining rescue services,

- g) of gas facility breakdown services,

- h) of emergency medical services and transport of the sick, injured and women in childbirth,

- i) of the armed forces used by military rescue units for fulfilling humanitarian tasks of civil protection.

(3) The Government may, by regulation, specify other vehicles which, for the performance of tasks related to the exercise of special duties, may be equipped with a special audible warning device supplemented by a special blue warning light.

(4) The driver of a vehicle with priority right of way may be a person over 21 years of age who must meet the conditions stipulated by this Act.

(5) The driver of a vehicle with priority right of way must not eat, drink or smoke while driving.

(6) Paragraphs 1 and 4 shall apply mutatis mutandis to drivers of vehicles accompanied in front, and if there are more than three vehicles, also in the rear, by vehicles of the armed forces or armed corps with priority right of way. Accompanied vehicles must have their side lights and dipped-beam headlights on.

(7) Drivers of other vehicles must allow vehicles with priority right of way and vehicles accompanied by them safe and smooth passage, and if necessary, also stop their vehicles in such a place so as not to obstruct them. Drivers of other vehicles must not join the group formed by vehicles with priority right of way and vehicles accompanied by them.

(8) If a special blue warning light is illuminated on a stationary vehicle, drivers of other vehicles must, depending on the circumstances, reduce their speed and, if necessary, stop their vehicle.

(9) In road traffic, it is prohibited to unlawfully use special warning lights and special audible warning signals used by a vehicle with priority right of way, or to imitate them.

p42.heading: Section 3
p42.text:
(1) The driver of a vehicle equipped with a special orange warning light^2) may use this light only if the safety of road traffic could be endangered by its driving or work activity. If the work activity of this vehicle requires it, its driver is not obliged to comply with the provisions of the road traffic rules listed in § 4 letter c), § 7 para 1 letter b), § 11 para 2, § 12 para 1, 2 and 4, § 13, § 14 para 1 and 2, § 18 para 2 letter b), § 22 para 3, § 24 para 4 letter a), c), d), e), f) and g), § 25 para 1, 2 and 3, § 26 para 3, § 27, § 28 para 2, § 30, 36, 37 and § 39 para 4 and 5; however, they are obliged to exercise increased caution so as not to endanger the safety of road traffic.

(2) Drivers of other vehicles must allow the vehicle according to paragraph 1 to drive or perform work and, depending on the circumstances, reduce their speed or even stop their vehicle.

(3) Groups (convoys) of vehicles of the armed forces or armed corps may be accompanied in front and in the rear by vehicles using a special orange warning light.^2) Accompanied vehicles must have their side lights and dipped-beam headlights on.


Special provisions for vehicle operation

p43.heading: Restriction on driving of certain vehicles
p43.text:
(1) On motorways and class I roads, it is prohibited for lorries and special vehicles^2) with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 7,500 kg, and for lorries and special vehicles^2) with a maximum permissible weight exceeding 3,500 kg with an attached trailer to drive

- a) on Sundays and other public holidays pursuant to a special legal regulation^19) (hereinafter referred to as a “public holiday”) from 0.00 to 22.00 hours,

- b) on Saturdays during the period from 1 July to 31 August from 7.00 to 20.00 hours.

(2) On class I roads outside municipalities, during the period from 15 April to 30 September, it is prohibited for special vehicles,^2) animal-drawn vehicles^2) and handcarts^2) with an overall width greater than 600 mm to drive

- a) on the last working day before a Saturday or a public holiday from 15.00 to 21.00 hours,

- b) on the first public holiday and on Saturday, if it follows a working day, from 7.00 to 11.00 hours,

- c) on the last public holiday from 15.00 to 21.00 hours.

(3) The driving prohibition pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 does not apply to vehicles used for

- a) combined transport of goods by rail or inland waterway and road from the consignor to the nearest combined transport transshipment point or from the nearest combined transport transshipment point to the consignee,

- b) essential agricultural seasonal transport,

- c) activities directly related to the ongoing maintenance, repair and construction of roads,

- d) transport of perishable goods pursuant to a special legal regulation,^20)

- e) transport of live animals,

- f) transport of fuels intended for the continuous supply of fuel stations,

- g) loading and unloading of aircraft, ships or railway wagons for a distance not exceeding 100 km,

- h) transport of postal items,

- i) driving without cargo, which is in connection with driving pursuant to letters a) to h),

- j) natural disaster,

- k) driving of vehicles of the armed forces, armed corps and fire and rescue services,

- l) transport of chemical substances subject to temperature changes or crystallization,

- m) driver training.

(4) Paragraphs 1 and 2 do not apply to vehicles equipped with a special blue light device and a special audible warning signal.

(5) The locally competent district authority may grant an exception from the driving prohibition pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 for reasons worthy of special consideration. Exceptions exceeding the scope of the district are granted by the Ministry of Transport and Communications (hereinafter referred to as the “Ministry”). The permit must be time-limited, but for a maximum period of one year.

(6) An implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the particulars of the application for an exception permit pursuant to paragraph 5.

p44.heading: Refuelling
p44.text:
(1) In the area of a fuel station, it is prohibited for both the driver and the transported person to smoke, handle open flames, or adjust or repair the vehicle's engine. Before refuelling, the driver must stop the engine and turn off the ignition. If an independent heater is used to heat the vehicle, the driver must turn it off before arriving at the fuel station.

(2) Drivers of vehicles with the right of way have priority when refuelling. They do not use special warning signals.

p45.heading: Obstruction of traffic on roads
p45.text:
(1) Anyone who has caused an obstruction to traffic on roads must remove it without delay; if they fail to do so, the owner of the road shall remove it at their expense.^1)

(2) If it is not possible to remove an obstruction to traffic on roads without delay, its originator must mark it and report it to the police.

(3) An obstruction to traffic on roads must be marked in such a way that another road user can notice it in time and from a sufficient distance, for example, with a red flag, traffic device “Barrier for marking a road closure”, “Directional board”, “Mobile road closure sign” or a vehicle equipped with a special orange or blue warning light. In reduced visibility, the traffic device must be supplemented with a yellow warning light. For marking a motor vehicle that is mandatorily equipped with a portable warning triangle,^2) § 26 para. 3 applies, and for marking a vehicle in reduced visibility, also § 33 para. 2 applies.

(4) If the obstruction to traffic on a road is a vehicle, a police officer decides on its removal; the vehicle shall be removed at the expense of its operator.

(5) Details on the marking of an obstruction to traffic on roads shall be regulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p46.heading: Stopping a vehicle in a tunnel
p46.text:
(1) If, while driving a vehicle in a tunnel, a vehicle breakdown occurs, rendering the vehicle inoperable, or if a traffic accident occurs, the driver is obliged to immediately

- a) turn off the engine, and if an independent heater is used to heat the vehicle, turn off this heater as well,

- b) take appropriate measures to prevent endangering the safety of traffic on roads in the tunnel; if circumstances require it, the driver is authorized to stop other vehicles,

- c) report the stopping of the vehicle or the standing of the vehicle to the police or to the person supervising tunnel operations.

(2) In the cases specified in paragraph 1, neither the driver nor the transported person may smoke or handle open flames.

(3) Transported persons in the vehicle are, in the cases specified in paragraph 1, obliged to leave the vehicle and gather in designated areas, or to leave the tunnel.

p47.heading: Traffic accident
p47.text:
(1) A "traffic accident" is an event in road traffic, such as a crash or collision, which occurred or commenced on a road and resulted in the death or injury of a person or damage to property in direct connection with the operation of a moving vehicle.

(2) A driver involved in a traffic accident is obliged to

- a) stop the vehicle immediately,

- b) refrain from consuming alcoholic beverages and other addictive substances after the accident for a period during which it would be detrimental to ascertain whether they consumed an alcoholic beverage or addictive substance before or during the drive.

(3) Participants in a traffic accident are obliged to

- a) take appropriate measures to prevent endangering the safety of road traffic at the scene of the traffic accident; if circumstances require, they are authorized to stop other vehicles,

- b) report the accident to the police in cases stipulated by this Act; if an injury occurred, provide first aid according to their abilities and call the emergency medical service for the injured person,

- c) mark the scene of the traffic accident,

- d) enable the resumption of road traffic, especially the operation of public transport vehicles,

- e) immediately report to the police any damage to the road, public utility equipment, or the environment, if it occurred during the traffic accident,

- f) upon request, prove their identity to each other and provide information about the vehicle involved in the traffic accident.

(4) If a traffic accident results in the death or injury of a person or material damage clearly exceeding 20,000 CZK on any of the vehicles involved, including transported items, or on other items, the participants in the traffic accident are obliged to

- a) immediately report the traffic accident to a police officer,

- b) refrain from actions that would be detrimental to a proper investigation of the traffic accident, especially moving vehicles; however, if the situation resulting from the traffic accident must change, especially if it is necessary for the extrication or treatment of an injured person or for the resumption of road traffic, particularly the operation of public transport vehicles, mark the situation and traces,

- c) remain at the scene of the traffic accident until the arrival of a police officer or return to this place immediately after providing or calling for assistance or reporting the traffic accident.

(5) If a traffic accident results in material damage to any of the vehicles involved, including transported items, or to other items, of less than 20,000 CZK, the participants in the traffic accident are obliged to report this accident to a police officer if they do not agree on fault or if the material damage was caused to the property of a third person.

Section 4

Transport of persons and cargo

p48.heading: Transport of persons
p48.text:
(1) The driver must not allow the number of transported persons older than 12 years to exceed the number of permitted seats designated for the transport of persons (hereinafter referred to as "permitted seat").

(2) In a motor vehicle or its trailer designated for the transport of persons,^2) persons may be transported in permitted seats only up to the permissible payload; however, the number of persons older than 12 years must not exceed the number of permitted seats.

(3) Paragraph 2 also applies to the transport of persons in the driver's cab of a truck.

(4) Persons younger than 15 years must not be transported in a special motor vehicle.

(5) In a trailer other than one designated for the transport of persons, the transport of persons, with the exception of cases under § 51, is prohibited.

p49.heading: Transport of persons by public transport vehicle
p49.text:
(1) A person waiting at a public transport vehicle stop, boarding this vehicle, being transported in it, or alighting from it, must behave in a manner that does not endanger the safety of road traffic.

(2) A person waiting at a public transport vehicle stop may enter the roadway at a stop without a boarding island only after the public transport vehicle has stopped at the stop.

(3) In the cases specified in paragraphs 1 and 2, the person is obliged to obey the instructions of the carrier.^21)

(4) If operational or other serious reasons require persons to enter the roadway when alighting from a public transport vehicle outside a stop or boarding it outside a stop, the carrier is authorized to stop other vehicles.

p50.heading: Transport of persons by taxi vehicle
p50.text:
(1) The authorization under § 49 para. 4 applies mutatis mutandis to the driver of a taxi vehicle.^21)

(2) Persons boarding and alighting from a taxi vehicle are obliged to follow the instructions of the driver of this vehicle.

p51.heading: Transport of persons in the cargo area of a truck and in the cargo area of a tractor trailer
p51.text:
(1) The transport of persons in the cargo area of a truck and in the cargo area of a tractor trailer is prohibited.

(2) Paragraph 1 does not apply to the transport of members of fire and rescue services, members of armed forces and armed corps in the performance of their duties, and other persons in the performance of civil protection duties and during natural disasters.

(3) Persons transported according to paragraph 2 must sit in the cargo area of the truck or in the cargo area of the tractor trailer on the floor or on seats firmly attached to the floor. Transported persons must not lean out, allow objects to protrude from the vehicle, or otherwise endanger the safety of road traffic while driving.

(4) The side walls of the cargo area of the truck and the cargo area of the tractor trailer must be sufficiently high to prevent transported persons from falling out while driving.

(5) The driver of a truck or tractor in whose cargo area persons are transported according to paragraph 2 may only be a driver older than 21 years who has at least two years of experience in driving this type of vehicle. These conditions do not apply to drivers of armed forces vehicles.

p52.heading: Transport of cargo
p52.text:
(1) Objects placed in a vehicle must be positioned so as not to restrict or endanger the driver or persons transported in the vehicle and not to obstruct the driver's view.

(2) When transporting cargo, the maximum permissible weight of the vehicle and the maximum permissible axle weight of the vehicle must not be exceeded. The cargo must be placed and secured on the vehicle in such a way as to ensure the stability and controllability of the vehicle and not to endanger the safety of road traffic, not to pollute or damage the road, not to cause excessive noise, not to pollute the air, and not to obscure the prescribed lighting, reflectors, and registration plate, national distinguishing mark, and indication of the maximum permitted speed; this also applies to devices used for securing and protecting the cargo, such as tarpaulins, chains, or ropes. Objects that can be easily overlooked, such as individual rods or pipes, must not protrude sideways.

(3) If the cargo protrudes from the vehicle by more than 1 m at the front or rear, or if the cargo protrudes sideways from a motor vehicle or a vehicle combination beyond the outer edge of the outline lights by more than 400 mm, and from a non-motorized vehicle beyond its edge by more than 400 mm, the protruding end of the cargo must be marked with a red flag measuring at least 300 x 300 mm; in reduced visibility, with a non-dazzling white light and a white reflector at the front, and a red light and a red reflector at the rear. Reflectors must not be triangular in shape and may be placed no more than 1.5 m above the road surface.

(4) When transporting agricultural products in conditions of unreduced visibility, paragraph 2 concerning the marking of cargo protruding sideways from the vehicle does not apply; in reduced visibility, lights, reflectors, or reflective panels shall be used.^2)

(5) When transporting live animals, the safety of the driver, transported persons, and animals, as well as the safety of road traffic, must not be endangered.

(6) When transporting loose substrates, the cargo must be secured to prevent its spontaneous dispersal.

(7) Loading and unloading cargo on a road is permitted only if it cannot be done off the road. Cargo must be unloaded and loaded as quickly as possible and in such a way as not to endanger the safety of road traffic.

(8) Further conditions for cargo transport, including conditions for the transport of dangerous goods, are stipulated by a special legal regulation.^21)

Section 5

Special provisions for walking, driving non-motorized vehicles, riding animals, and leading and herding animals

Walking

p53.text:
(1) A pedestrian must primarily use the pavement or a pedestrian path. A pedestrian carrying an object that could endanger traffic on the pavement shall use the right verge or the right edge of the roadway.

(2) Road users other than pedestrians may not use the pavement or pedestrian path, unless otherwise stipulated in this Act.

(3) Where there is no pavement or if it is impassable, pedestrians shall walk on the left verge, and where there is no verge or if it is impassable, they shall walk as close as possible to the left edge of the roadway. Pedestrians may walk on the verge or along the edge of the roadway no more than two abreast. In reduced visibility, increased road traffic, or in dangerous and unclear sections, pedestrians may only walk in single file.

(4) If a pedestrian and cycle path marked with the traffic sign „Pedestrian and Cycle Path“ is established, a pedestrian must not endanger a cyclist riding on the path.

(5) If a pedestrian and cycle path marked with the traffic sign „Pedestrian and Cycle Path“ is established, on which a lane for pedestrians and a lane for cyclists are separated, the pedestrian is obliged to use only the lane designated for pedestrians. The pedestrian may use the lane designated for cyclists only when bypassing, entering, and exiting the pedestrian and cycle path; in doing so, they must not endanger cyclists riding in the lane designated for cyclists.

(6) A person moving with the aid of a manual or motorized wheelchair for the disabled must not endanger other pedestrians on the pavement or pedestrian path. If they cannot use the pavement, they may use the right verge or the right edge of the roadway.

(7) A person leading a bicycle or moped may use the pavement only if they do not endanger other pedestrians; otherwise, they must use the right verge or the right edge of the roadway.

(8) A person moving on skis, roller skates, or similar sports equipment must not endanger other pedestrians on the pavement or pedestrian path.

p54.text:
(1) If a controlled intersection, a pedestrian crossing, a place for crossing the roadway, an overpass, or an underpass marked with the traffic sign „Pedestrian Crossing“, „Underpass or Overpass“ is closer than 50 m, the pedestrian must cross only at these places. At a pedestrian crossing, one walks on the right.

(2) Outside a pedestrian crossing, it is permitted to cross the roadway only perpendicularly to its axis. Before entering the roadway, the pedestrian must ascertain whether they can cross the roadway without endangering themselves or other road users. A pedestrian may cross the roadway only if, considering the distance and speed of approaching vehicles, they do not force their drivers to suddenly change direction or speed.

(3) As soon as a pedestrian enters a pedestrian crossing or the roadway, they must not stop or linger there without reason. A blind pedestrian signals the intention to cross the roadway by waving a white cane in the direction of crossing. A pedestrian must not enter a pedestrian crossing or the roadway if vehicles with priority of passage are approaching; if they are on a pedestrian crossing or the roadway, they must immediately clear the space for these vehicles to pass. A pedestrian must not enter a pedestrian crossing or the roadway immediately in front of an approaching vehicle. A pedestrian must give way to a tram.

(4) A pedestrian must not climb over railings or other barriers on the roadway.

p55.text:
(1) Before a railway crossing, a pedestrian must proceed with particular caution, and must especially ascertain whether they can cross the railway crossing safely.

(2) A pedestrian must not enter a railway crossing in cases stipulated in § 29 para. 1 letter a) to e).

(3) In cases referred to in § 29 para. 1 letter a), b) and c), a pedestrian may cross a railway crossing only if they have received oral consent from an authorized employee of the railway operator^11) before the railway crossing. In this case, the pedestrian is obliged to follow the instructions of the authorized employee of the railway operator when crossing the railway crossing. The authorized employee of the railway operator is obliged to present a valid authorization from the railway operator upon request by the pedestrian.

p56.heading: Pedestrian formations
p56.text:
(1) For an organized pedestrian formation, such as members of the armed forces, schoolchildren, or a procession, the obligations of a driver under § 5 para. 1 letter b), § 11 para. 1, § 12 to 16, § 20 to 24, § 25 para. 1 and 2, § 27, § 28 para. 1, 2 and 5, § 29 and 30 apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) In reduced visibility, an organized pedestrian formation must be marked at the front on both sides with a non-dazzling white light and at the rear on both sides with a non-dazzling red light. The marking with lights may be replaced by clothing accessories with retroreflective material marking.

(3) An organized pedestrian formation on a bridge must not march in unison.

(4) The leader of the formation, who may only be a person older than 15 years and sufficiently competent for this purpose, is responsible for compliance with the obligations under paragraphs 1 to 3.

(5) An organized pedestrian formation walking at most in two abreast may walk on the pavement, on the right; it does not need to be marked according to paragraph 2.

(6) For an organized group of children who are not yet subject to compulsory school attendance, the provisions for pedestrians apply.

(7) The leader of an organized formation of schoolchildren or an organized group of children who are not yet subject to compulsory school attendance is authorized to stop vehicles when crossing the roadway.

(8) The design and method of use of clothing accessories with retroreflective material marking shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

Cycling

p57.text:
(1) If a cycle lane, cycle path is established, or if a cycle lane and a designated area for cyclists are established at a controlled intersection, the cyclist is obliged to use them.

(2) On the roadway, a bicycle is ridden along the right edge of the roadway; if pedestrians are neither endangered nor restricted thereby, it may be ridden on the right shoulder. For the purposes of road traffic, a scooter is also considered a bicycle.

(3) Cyclists may ride only in single file.

(4) If vehicles are moving slowly or standing in a queue along the right edge of the roadway, a cyclist traveling in the same direction may overtake or pass these vehicles from the right side along the right edge of the roadway or shoulder, if there is sufficient space to the right of the vehicles; in doing so, they are obliged to exercise increased caution.

(5) If a pedestrian and cycle path marked with the traffic sign „Pedestrian and Cycle Path“ is established, the cyclist must not endanger pedestrians walking on the path.

(6) If a pedestrian and cycle path marked with the traffic sign „Pedestrian and Cycle Path“ is established, on which a lane for pedestrians and a lane for cyclists are separated, the cyclist is obliged to use only the lane designated for cyclists. The cyclist may use the lane designated for pedestrians only when passing around, overtaking, turning, branching off, and entering the pedestrian and cycle path; in doing so, they must not endanger pedestrians walking in the lane designated for pedestrians.

(7) A cycle lane or cycle path may also be used by a person moving on skis or roller skates or similar sports equipment. In doing so, this person is obliged to follow the rules according to paragraphs 3, 5 and 6 and the light signals according to § 73.

p58.text:
(1) A cyclist under 15 years of age is obliged to use a protective helmet of an approved type according to a special legal regulation^2) while riding, and to have it properly fitted and fastened on their head.

(2) A child under 10 years of age may ride a bicycle on a road, local road, and publicly accessible utility road^1) only under the supervision of a person older than 15 years; this does not apply to riding on a pavement, cycle path, and in a residential and pedestrian zone.

(3) It is not permitted to ride two people on a single-seat bicycle; however, if the bicycle is equipped with an auxiliary seat for transporting a child and fixed footrests, a person older than 15 years may carry a person younger than 7 years.

(4) A cyclist must not ride without holding the handlebars, hold onto another vehicle, lead a second bicycle, handcart, dog or other animal while riding, or carry objects that would hinder the steering of the bicycle or endanger other road users. While riding, the cyclist must have their feet on the pedals.

(5) A trailer may be attached to a bicycle, which is not wider than 800 mm, has two non-triangular red reflectors at the rear placed as close as possible to the side outlines of the trailer, and is connected to the bicycle by a rigid coupling device. If the trailer or its load obscures the bicycle's rear red outline light in reduced visibility, the trailer must be equipped with a non-dazzling red light on the left rear.

p59.heading: Riding with animal-drawn vehicles and handcarts
p59.text:
(1) Only a person older than 15 years may be a driver of an animal-drawn vehicle.

(2) If the driver of an animal-drawn vehicle is not riding on the vehicle, they must walk on its right side while the animal-drawn vehicle is in motion. Before leaving the animal-drawn vehicle, the driver must brake the vehicle, release the inner traces, and block the wheels on a slope.

(3) In the period from November 1 to March 31, and in reduced visibility in the period from April 1 to October 31, the animal-drawn vehicle must be marked at the front with at least one non-dazzling white light on the left side and at the rear with at least one non-dazzling red light on the left side.

(4) A person pulling or pushing a handcart with an overall width greater than 600 mm must walk along the right edge of the roadway; if pedestrians are not endangered or restricted thereby, they may walk on the right shoulder. In reduced visibility, this person or the cart must be marked on the left side with a non-dazzling white light.

(5) On Class I and II roads^1), driving with animal-drawn vehicles and handcarts specified in paragraph 4 is prohibited from 23:00 to 4:00.

p60.heading: Riding animals and leading and driving animals
p60.text:
(1) For riders on animals and for escorts of led and driven animals, the obligations of a driver pursuant to § 5 para 1 letter b), d), f), g) and h), § 11 para 1, § 12 to 17, § 20 to 24, § 25 para 1 and 2, § 27, § 28 para 1, 2, 4 and 5, § 29, 30, § 41 para 7 and § 42 para 2 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(2) If a lane for riders on animals or a path for riders on animals marked with the traffic sign „Path for riders on animals“ is established, the rider on an animal must use this lane or path.

(3) Other road users may not use the lane for riders on animals or the path for riders on animals.

(4) On the roadway, the rider on an animal must ride along the right edge of the roadway; if pedestrians are not endangered or restricted thereby, they may ride on the right shoulder.

(5) On a road, local road, and publicly accessible účelová komunikace, only a rider older than 15 years may ride an animal. A person older than 12 years may ride an animal on a road, local road, and publicly accessible účelová komunikace only under the supervision of a person older than 15 years. Riders on animals may ride only in single file. A rider may lead only one animal.

(6) The escort of led or driven animals must be a person older than 15 years. If the safety of road traffic requires it, the escort of led or driven animals is authorized to stop vehicles when accompanying animals.

(7) Animals may be led or driven at walking speed and only in such a way that road traffic is not endangered and is restricted as little as possible.

(8) Driven animals in a herd must be accompanied by the necessary number of escorts of driven animals and must be divided into suitably long groups separated by sufficiently large gaps.

(9) The escort of led animals may lead a maximum of two animals, which must be harnessed together.

(10) In reduced visibility, the rider on an animal must be marked on the left side with a non-dazzling white light visible from the front and a red non-dazzling light visible from the rear. The escort of led and driven animals must be marked with a non-dazzling white light in reduced visibility. From dusk to dawn, it is forbidden to use class I or II roads for riding animals and leading animals.^1)

Part 4

Regulation and control of road traffic

Section 1

Regulation of road traffic

p61.heading: General, local and temporary regulation of road traffic
p61.text:
(1) The general regulation of road traffic is stipulated by this Act.

(2) Local regulation of road traffic is the regulation of road traffic implemented by traffic signs, light signals, possibly also accompanying acoustic signals, or traffic devices.

(3) Temporary regulation of road traffic is the regulation of road traffic implemented by portable vertical traffic signs, temporary horizontal traffic signs, light signals, and traffic devices.

(4) Temporary regulation of road traffic and the use of operational information devices for selected or repeated activities related to the administration, maintenance, measurement, repairs, or construction of a road or to ensure road traffic safety may be stipulated by general schemes. The validity of a general scheme must be time-limited, however, for a maximum period of one year.

p62.heading: Traffic Signs
p62.text:
(1) Vertical and horizontal traffic signs are distinguished.

(2) Vertical traffic signs are permanent, variable, and portable. A variable vertical traffic sign is a traffic sign whose active surface can change. A portable vertical traffic sign means a traffic sign placed on a red and white striped pole (stand) or on a vehicle.

(3) Horizontal traffic signs are permanent and temporary. Horizontal traffic signs may be supplemented by road studs.

(4) The shapes of traffic sign symbols must not be changed; this does not apply to traffic signs with symbols that can be reversed, and with symbols that are listed only as patterns, and to variable vertical traffic signs.

(5) The implementing legal regulation stipulates the meaning, use, design, and shapes of traffic signs and their symbols.

(6) Traffic signs, light and acoustic signals, traffic devices, and operational information devices must comply with special technical regulations^22) in terms of their dimensions, colors, and technical requirements.

p63.heading: Vertical Traffic Signs

p64.heading: Horizontal traffic signs
p64.text: Horizontal traffic signs are used independently or in conjunction with vertical traffic signs, or with traffic devices, whose meaning they emphasize or specify. Horizontal traffic signs are marked with color or another comprehensible method; a temporary change in local road traffic regulation is marked with yellow or orange color.

p65.heading: Light signals, accompanying acoustic signals, and warning lights
p65.text:
(1) Light signals, and potentially accompanying acoustic signals, regulate road traffic or alert to the necessity of exercising increased caution.
