(2) The design and shapes of symbols of light signals and the character of acoustic signals made in accordance with the implementing legal regulation must not be changed; this does not apply to light signals with symbols that can be reversed, and with symbols that are listed only as patterns. Accompanying acoustic signals used for regulating road traffic must not be used for any other purpose.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the types, meaning, use, design, and shapes of symbols of light and acoustic signals.

(4) Light signals must comply with special technical regulations^22) in terms of their dimensions, colors, and technical requirements.

p66.heading: Traffic devices
p66.text:
(1) Traffic devices supplement traffic signs and light and acoustic signals, direct road traffic, and protect road users.

(2) The design and shapes of symbols of traffic devices made in accordance with the implementing legal regulation must not be changed; this does not apply to traffic devices with symbols that can be reversed, and with symbols that are listed only as patterns.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the types, meaning, use, design, and shapes of traffic devices.

(4) Traffic devices must comply with special technical regulations^22) in terms of their dimensions, colors, and technical requirements.

p67.heading: Special markings for vehicles and persons
p67.text:
(1) The marking of a vehicle transporting a severely disabled person or a person with severe mobility impairment (hereinafter referred to as „marking O 1“) or the marking of a vehicle driven by a hearing-impaired person (hereinafter referred to as „marking O 2“) may only be used by persons who receive this marking from the relevant district authority or from an authorized body abroad.

(2) Marking O 1 shall be issued by the district authority to a person who has been granted extraordinary benefits of the II. degree, with the exception of those affected by complete or practical deafness, or of the III. degree according to a special legal regulation.^24) Marking O 2 shall be issued by the district authority to the owner of a motor vehicle who has been granted extraordinary benefits of the II. degree due to complete or practical deafness.

(3) In individual cases, and if urgently necessary, drivers with marking O 1 do not have to observe the no-standing prohibition and the no-standing prohibition resulting from the traffic sign „No Standing“ for the necessary period; the safety and smooth flow of road traffic must not be endangered thereby.

(4) Entry to a reserved parking space for a vehicle marked with sign O 1 is prohibited for vehicles without marking O 1.

(5) A person who has been issued marking O 1 may request the establishment of a reserved parking space at their place of residence according to a special legal regulation.^25)

(6) Blind and deafblind persons use canes as special devices.

(7) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the types and designs of special markings for vehicles and persons.

p68.heading: Devices for operational information
p68.text:
(1) Devices for operational information display current data that are significant for the safety and smooth flow of road traffic, for example, accident, smog, travel time to a specified destination, orientation to a park-and-ride facility, road or air temperature.

(2) The design and shapes of symbols of devices for operational information may change; this does not apply if traffic sign symbols, light signals, or traffic devices are used on operational information boards; the provision of § 62 para. 4 is not affected by this.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the meaning, use, design, and shapes of symbols intended for use on devices for operational information.

(4) Devices for operational information must comply with special technical regulations^22) in terms of their dimensions, colors, and technical requirements.

Section 2

p69.heading: Regulation of road traffic
p69.text:
Road traffic is regulated by light signals, and potentially accompanying acoustic signals, or by instructions from a police officer or persons authorized to regulate road traffic according to § 75 para. 5 of this Act or a special legal regulation.^26)

Regulation of traffic by light signals

p70.text:
(1) When controlling traffic at an intersection, three-color signal systems with full signals or directional signals are primarily used.

(2) When controlling traffic at an intersection, for drivers, this means:

- a) a signal with a red light „Stop!“ means the obligation to stop the vehicle before the traffic sign „Solid stop line“, „Solid stop line with Give Way symbol!“ and „Solid stop line with STOP inscription“, and where such a traffic sign is not present, before the traffic light signal device,

- b) a signal with simultaneously lit red and yellow lights „Caution!“ means the obligation to prepare to drive,

- c) a signal with a green full circular light „Go“ means the possibility to proceed, and if the provisions on turning are observed, the driver may turn right or left, while having to give way to pedestrians crossing in the free direction on a pedestrian crossing. If the signal „Signal for leaving the intersection“ located in the opposite corner of the intersection is lit, § 21 (5) does not apply to turning left,

- d) a signal with a yellow light „Caution!“ means the obligation to stop the vehicle before the traffic sign „Solid stop line“, „Solid stop line with Give Way symbol!“ and „Solid stop line with STOP inscription“, and where such a traffic sign is not present, before the traffic light signal device; however, if the vehicle is already so close when this signal lights up that the driver could not stop the vehicle safely, the driver may proceed. If the light of this signal flashes intermittently, it is not an intersection with traffic controlled by traffic light signals,

- e) a signal with a green directional arrow or arrows (for example, „Signal for straight direction“, „Combined signal for straight direction and turning right“) means the possibility to proceed only in the direction indicated by the arrow or arrows. If the green arrow points left, § 21 (5) does not apply to turning left,

- f) the signal „Yellow light signal in the shape of a pedestrian“, which supplements a signal with a green arrow pointing right or left, warns drivers that when driving in the direction indicated by this arrow, they are crossing the path of pedestrians,

- g) the signal „Supplementary green arrow“ lit simultaneously with a signal with a red light „Stop!“ or with a yellow light „Caution!“ means the possibility to proceed only in the direction indicated by the arrow or arrows; in doing so, the driver must give way to vehicles and riders on animals traveling in the free direction and to groups of pedestrians walking in the free direction; in doing so, the driver must not endanger or restrict crossing pedestrians.

(3) When controlling traffic outside an intersection, for example before a pedestrian crossing or before a blind spot, paragraph 2 applies mutatis mutandis.

p71.text:
(1) If the signal „Speed signal with variable signal symbol“ or „Speed signal with multiple signal symbols“ is used, the illuminated number indicates the recommended speed in km.h^-1.

(2) If the signal „No entry for vehicles into the lane“ is lit above a lane, the driver must not use this lane. If the signal „Free entry for vehicles into the lane“ is lit above a lane, the driver may use this lane.

(3) If the signal „Light arrow left“ or „Light arrow right“ is lit, the driver must leave the lane or bypass the obstacle in the indicated direction.

(4) When controlling traffic during road closures, road works, or for other reasons, portable signal devices of a three-color or two-color system with full circular lights are used. The provisions of § 70 (2) apply mutatis mutandis.

(5) In special cases, for example, to secure the entry of a tram onto a road, the signal „Stop!“ is used, preceded by the signal „Caution!“; the driver must stop before the traffic sign „Solid stop line“, „Solid stop line with Give Way symbol!“ and „Solid stop line with STOP inscription“, and where such a traffic sign is not present, before the traffic light signal device.

(6) A signal of two alternately flashing red lights placed side by side means for the driver the obligation to stop the vehicle before the traffic light signal device.

(7) If the traffic light signal device is not equipped with the signal „Caution!“, the first sentence of § 70 (2) (d) applies mutatis mutandis when the signal to stop the vehicle lights up.

(8) The signal „Flashing yellow light“ used alone warns drivers of the need to exercise increased caution.

(9) The signal „Flashing yellow light“ used together with a traffic sign or traffic device emphasizes the meaning of the traffic sign or traffic device.

p72.text: Tram traffic is controlled by a traffic light signal device with „Signals for trams“. These signals may also be used on lanes reserved for city public transport buses or trolleybuses.

p73.text:
(1) If a traffic light signal device with the light signals „Signal for cyclists with Stop sign!“, „Signal for cyclists with Caution sign!“, „Signal for cyclists with Go sign!“ is installed on a cycle path or cycle lane, § 70 (2) (a) to (d) applies mutatis mutandis. This also applies if the signal with full circular lights is supplemented by a white panel with a bicycle image.

(2) If the signals for cyclists are located beyond the road, the signal means:

- a) „Signal for cyclists with Stop sign!“ that the cyclist must not enter the roadway,

- b) „Signal for cyclists with Stop sign!“ together with the signal „Signal for cyclists with Caution sign!“ that the cyclist is obliged to prepare to ride,

- c) „Signal for cyclists with Go sign!“ that the cyclist may cross the roadway; if the signal „Signal for cyclists with Caution sign!“ then lights up, the cyclist may complete crossing to the traffic light signal device with this signal.

(3) Where traffic light signal devices for cyclists are equipped with a button for cyclists, the cyclist may, after pressing the button, enter the roadway only upon the sign of the signal „Signal for cyclists with Go sign!“.

p74.text:
(1) The following two-colour system signals, or accompanying acoustic signals, shall be used for pedestrians, meaning:

- a) “Pedestrian signal with a Free sign”, that the pedestrian may cross the road; if the “Pedestrian signal with a Stop sign!” then lights up, the pedestrian may complete crossing to the traffic light signalling device with this signal,

- b) “Pedestrian signal with a Stop sign!”, that the pedestrian may not enter the road.

(2) Where pedestrian traffic light signalling devices are equipped with a pedestrian button, the pedestrian may only enter the road after pressing the button upon the signal “Pedestrian signal with a Free sign”.

p75.heading: Traffic control by police officer's instructions
p75.text:
(1) A police officer controls traffic on roads by changing posture and arm signals; they usually use a directional baton held in their right hand. Their instructions mean for drivers and pedestrians:

- a) “Stop!” for the direction towards which the police officer is facing or has their back; the driver is obliged to stop the vehicle,

- b) “Caution!”, if the police officer raises their arm or the forearm of their right arm with the directional baton; a driver travelling from a direction for which traffic was previously stopped is obliged to prepare to drive; a driver travelling in a previously free direction is obliged to stop the vehicle; however, if they are already so close that they could not safely stop the vehicle, they may continue driving,

- c) “Free” for the direction towards which the police officer is sideways; the driver may continue driving, and if they comply with the provisions on turning, they may turn right or left,

- d) if the police officer has their right arm extended forward and their left arm extended sideways, it means “Stop!” for drivers approaching towards the police officer's back and right side, and “Free” for drivers approaching towards the police officer's left side; a driver approaching towards the police officer's back and right side is obliged to stop the vehicle; a driver approaching the police officer's left side may continue driving, and if they comply with the provisions on turning, they may turn right or left; a driver approaching towards the police officer's front may only turn right; pedestrians may only cross the road behind the police officer's back.

(2) If a driver stops at an intersection when controlling traffic according to paragraph 1 letter a), b) and d), they are obliged to stop before the intersection boundary.

(3) For the instructions “Stop!” and “Free”, the police officer may extend both arms or one arm sideways; they may bring both arms to their sides if the posture is sufficient for traffic control. This does not apply to the instruction specified in paragraph 1 letter d).

(4) In addition to the instructions specified in paragraph 1, a police officer may give other instructions when controlling traffic, for example, “Accelerate!” by horizontally waving their arm across the centre of their body or “Slow down!” by waving their arm up and down. To emphasize their instruction, the police officer may use a whistle signal.

(5) Paragraphs 1 to 4 also apply to traffic control on roads by a military police officer and a member of the military order service, who uses a directional baton or a stop disc when controlling traffic on roads.

(6) Upon request, a member of the military order service, when controlling traffic on roads, is obliged to present a valid authorization for traffic control on roads to a police officer or military police officer.

(7) The uniform and designation of a member of the military order service when controlling traffic on roads, the model of the directional baton and stop disc, the form and content of the authorization for traffic control on roads according to paragraph 6, and the model of instructions for traffic control on roads according to paragraphs 1 and 4 shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

Section 3

Relationships between traffic regulations on roads, their establishment and the use of traffic signs, light signals, traffic devices and traffic information

p76.heading: Relationships between general, local and temporary traffic regulations on roads
p76.text:
(1) Local traffic regulations on roads take precedence over general traffic regulations on roads. Temporary traffic regulations on roads take precedence over both local and general traffic regulations on roads.

(2) Vertical permanent traffic signs take precedence over horizontal traffic signs.

(3) Temporary horizontal traffic signs take precedence over permanent horizontal traffic signs.

(4) Portable vertical traffic signs take precedence over all traffic signs.

(5) Instructions from a police officer, military police officer or a member of the military order service take precedence over light signals, traffic signs or traffic devices.

(6) Light signals take precedence over vertical traffic signs regulating priority.

(7) If a horizontal traffic sign “Pedestrian crossing”, “Cyclist crossing”, “Bus or trolleybus stop”, “No standing”, “No stopping” or “Diagonal parallel lines” is used, this horizontal traffic sign at the place of use takes precedence over the vertical traffic sign “Parking” or the vertical traffic sign “Zone with traffic restrictions” with a symbol regulating stopping, standing or parking.

p77.heading: Establishment of local and temporary traffic regulations on roads
p77.text:
(1) Local and temporary traffic management on roads and the use of operational information devices shall be determined by

- a) on a road, other than an expressway, and a local road^1) by the competent district authority after prior written opinion from the competent police authority, and, if it is a local road, also after prior opinion from the competent municipal authority,

- b) on a motorway^1) and an expressway^1) by the Ministry after prior written opinion from the Ministry of the Interior.

(2) Local and temporary traffic management on roads and operational information devices shall be determined on a publicly accessible service road by the owner with the consent of the competent district authority and after prior written opinion from the competent police authority.

(3) The use of traffic signs „Railway crossing with barriers“, „Railway crossing without barriers“, „Advance warning board“, „Tram“, „Give way to tram!“ and the use of the traffic sign „STOP, give way!“, if it is to be placed before a railway crossing, shall be determined on a road and local road by the competent district authority after prior written opinion from the competent police authority and after consultation with the competent railway authority.^11)

(4) The use of traffic signs „Warning cross for single-track railway crossing“ and „Warning cross for multi-track railway crossing“, the light signal „Signal for securing railway crossing“ and signals for trams shall be determined by the competent railway authority^11) after consultation with the competent district authority and after prior written opinion from the competent police authority.

p78.heading: Use of traffic signs, light signals, traffic devices and operational information
p78.text:
(1) Traffic signs, light and acoustic signals, traffic devices and operational information devices must form a coherent system.

(2) Traffic signs, light and acoustic signals, traffic devices and operational information devices may only be used to such an extent and in such a manner as is strictly required by the safety and fluidity of traffic on roads or by another important public interest.

(3) Portable vertical traffic signs, temporary horizontal traffic signs, light signals, traffic devices and operational information devices may only be used for the absolutely necessary period and only for the reasons stated in paragraph 2.

(4) Variable vertical traffic signs are used primarily in a road traffic management system that reacts to specific situations in road traffic or weather conditions.

(5) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the manner and extent of use of portable vertical traffic signs, temporary horizontal traffic signs, light signals, traffic devices and operational information devices.

(6) In the immediate vicinity of a road in a municipality, it is prohibited to place anything that could be mistaken for a traffic sign, light and acoustic signal, traffic device or traffic information device, or that could reduce their visibility, recognisability or effectiveness, dazzle road users or distract their attention in a manner affecting road safety.

Section 4

Stopping vehicles

p79.text:
(1) Vehicles may be stopped by

- a) a police officer in uniform,

- b) a military police officer in uniform,

- c) a municipal police officer in uniform in cases according to a special legal regulation^27) and before a pedestrian crossing to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth over a road in the vicinity of a school facility or during an organised event,

- d) an employee of a railway operator in the vicinity of a railway crossing,

- e) a participant in a traffic accident, if circumstances under § 47 require it,

- f) a carrier according to § 49 paragraph 4,

- g) the leader of an organised group of school youth, the leader of an organised group of children who are not yet subject to compulsory schooling, and the escort of disabled persons when crossing the roadway,

- h) the escort of led or driven animals according to § 60 paragraph 6,

- i) an employee performing work related to the administration, maintenance, measurement, repairs and construction of a road and to ensure road safety, marked according to the implementing legal regulation,

- j) a person authorised by the district authority to ensure the safe crossing of children and school youth over a road in the vicinity of a school facility (hereinafter referred to as „authorised person“); the authorised person is obliged to be marked according to the implementing legal regulation when performing their activity.

(2) A vehicle is stopped by giving a signal to stop the vehicle. The persons listed in paragraph 1 give a signal to stop the vehicle by raising an arm or with a stop disc, and in reduced visibility with a red light moved in an upper semicircle. From a moving vehicle, this signal is given by waving an arm up and down or with an extended stop disc. A police officer may also stop vehicles from a moving vehicle by illuminating the inscription „STOP“ according to a special legal regulation.^13)

(3) The signal to stop a vehicle must be given in a timely and clear manner with regard to the circumstances of road traffic so that the driver can safely stop the vehicle and so that road safety is not endangered.

(4) An authorised person may only be a person over 18 years of age who is sufficiently qualified for the activity according to paragraph 1 letter j).

(5) When performing the activity according to paragraph 1 letter j), the authorised person must carry an authorisation issued by the district authority. The authorisation must be time-limited, for a maximum period of one year. Upon request, the authorised person is obliged to present a valid authorisation to a police officer.

(6) An authorised person may stop vehicles only at a pedestrian crossing; where there is no pedestrian crossing or it is impassable, they may also stop vehicles at another place on the roadway.

(7) An authorised person may not stop vehicles in the vicinity of an intersection with controlled traffic, not even at a pedestrian crossing.

(8) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the manner of marking persons according to paragraph 1 letters i) and j) and the template for the authorisation according to paragraph 1 letter j).

p80.text: A driving licence authorises its holder to drive a motor vehicle classified in the relevant group or subgroup of driving licences.

p81.heading: Driving licence groups and subgroups
p81.text:
(1) Driving licences for motor vehicles are divided into groups and subgroups.

(2) A Group A driving licence authorises the driving of motorcycles with or without a sidecar.

(3) A Subgroup A1 driving licence authorises the driving of light motorcycles with a cylinder capacity not exceeding 125 cm^3 and a power output not exceeding 11 kW.

(4) A Group B driving licence authorises the driving of

- a) motor vehicles, with the exception of vehicles referred to in paragraphs 2, 3 and 15 and motorcycles with a power output exceeding 25 kW or with a power-to-weight ratio exceeding 0.16 kW/kg or motorcycles with a sidecar and a power-to-weight ratio exceeding 0.16 kW/kg, whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 3,500 kg and with a maximum of 8 seats, excluding the driver's seat; a trailer with a maximum authorised mass not exceeding 750 kg may be attached to such a motor vehicle,

- b) tractors and self-propelled working machines, whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 3,500 kg,

- c) vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle according to letter a) or b) and a trailer, provided that the maximum authorised mass of the combination does not exceed 3,500 kg and the maximum authorised mass of the trailer does not exceed the „unladen mass“^2) of the motor vehicle.

(5) A Subgroup B1 driving licence authorises the driving of motor tricycles and quadricycles referred to in paragraph 4, whose maximum design speed exceeds 45 km.h^-1 or are powered by an internal combustion engine with a cylinder capacity exceeding 50 cm^3 or are powered by any other device of comparable power. The unladen mass of these vehicles must not exceed 550 kg; the mass of batteries is not included in the unladen mass of an electrically powered vehicle.

(6) A Group C driving licence authorises the driving of motor vehicles, with the exception of vehicles referred to in paragraphs 8 and 9, whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 3,500 kg; a trailer whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 750 kg may be attached to such a motor vehicle.

(7) A Subgroup C1 driving licence authorises the driving of motor vehicles, with the exception of vehicles referred to in paragraphs 8 and 9, whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 7,500 kg; a trailer whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 750 kg may be attached to such a motor vehicle.

(8) A Group D driving licence authorises the driving of motor vehicles designed for the carriage of persons with more than 8 seats, excluding the driver's seat; a trailer whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 750 kg may be attached to such a motor vehicle.

(9) A Subgroup D1 driving licence authorises the driving of motor vehicles designed for the carriage of persons with more than 8 seats, excluding the driver's seat, but not more than 16 seats, excluding the driver's seat; a trailer whose maximum authorised mass does not exceed 750 kg may be attached to such a motor vehicle.

(10) A Group B + E driving licence authorises the driving of vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle referred to in paragraph 4 and a trailer, unless it is a vehicle combination according to paragraph 4 letter c).

(11) A Group C + E driving licence authorises the driving of vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle referred to in paragraph 6 and a trailer whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 750 kg.

(12) A Subgroup C1 + E driving licence authorises the driving of vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle referred to in paragraph 7 and a trailer whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 750 kg. However, the maximum authorised mass of the combination must not exceed 12,000 kg and the maximum authorised mass of the trailer must not exceed the unladen mass of the motor vehicle.

(13) A Group D + E driving licence authorises the driving of vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle referred to in paragraph 8 and a trailer whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 750 kg.

(14) A Subgroup D1 + E driving licence authorises the driving of vehicle combinations composed of a motor vehicle referred to in paragraph 9 and a trailer whose maximum authorised mass exceeds 750 kg and in which no persons are carried. However, the maximum authorised mass of the combination must not exceed 12,000 kg and the maximum authorised mass of the trailer must not exceed the unladen mass of the motor vehicle.

(15) A Group AM driving licence authorises the driving of mopeds and light motorcycles with a maximum design speed of 45 km.h^-1.

(16) A Group T driving licence authorises the driving of tractors and self-propelled working machines; a trailer may be attached to the motor vehicle.

p82.heading: Conditions for granting and holding a driving licence
p82.text:
(1) A driving licence may only be granted to a person who

- a) has reached the age stipulated by this Act,

- b) is medically fit to drive motor vehicles,

- c) is professionally competent to drive motor vehicles,

- d) has fulfilled other conditions stipulated by this Act.

(2) The conditions under paragraph 1 letters b) to d) must be met by the holder of a driving licence of the relevant group or subgroup for the entire period of holding the driving licence.

p83.heading: Age
p83.text:
(1) A driving licence may only be granted to a person older than:

- a) 15 years, in the case of a driving licence of group AM,

- b) 16 years, in the case of a driving licence of subgroup A1,

- c) 17 years, in the case of a driving licence of group T and subgroup B1,

- d) 18 years, in the case of a driving licence of groups A, B, B+E, C, C+E and subgroups C1 and C1+E,

- e) 21 years, in the case of a driving licence of groups A, D, D+E and subgroups D1, D1+E.

(2) A driving licence of group A granted to a person pursuant to paragraph 1 letter d) only authorises the driving of motorcycles with an engine power of up to 25 kW or with a power/weight ratio not exceeding 0.16 kW/kg, or motorcycles with a sidecar and with a power/weight ratio not exceeding 0.16 kW/kg.

(3) A driving licence of group A granted to a person pursuant to paragraph 1 letter e) authorises the driving of motorcycles with an engine power exceeding 25 kW or with a power/weight ratio exceeding 0.16 kW/kg, or motorcycles with a sidecar and with a power/weight ratio exceeding 0.16 kW/kg.

(4) A driving licence for driving motor vehicles may only be granted to a person specified in paragraph 1 letters a) to c) with the written consent of their legal guardian.

(5) The Ministry shall grant an exemption from the age requirement for a driving licence of group A and subgroup A1 to a person who holds a motorsport athlete's licence, based on their written application, or the written application of their legal guardian. The granted exemption is valid only for driving in sports competitions.

Medical fitness to drive motor vehicles

p84.text:
(1) Medical fitness to drive motor vehicles means the physical and mental ability to drive motor vehicles (hereinafter referred to as “medical fitness”).

(2) Medical fitness is assessed and a medical fitness report (hereinafter referred to as “report”) is issued by the assessing physician on the basis of the declaration of the applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence, the result of a medical examination, and other necessary specialist examinations.

(3) A person is not medically fit to drive a motor vehicle if they have behavioural disorders caused by addiction to alcohol or other psychoactive substances according to the medical fitness report.

(4) For the purposes of this Act, the assessing physician means:

- a) the registered general practitioner,

- b) a physician of an occupational health service facility,

- c) any general practitioner for a person who does not have a registered general practitioner or a physician of an occupational health service facility.

(5) The registered general practitioner is obliged to provide the physician of the occupational health service facility, upon their request, with an extract from the medical documentation containing data essential for determining medical fitness.

(6) For a person who does not have a registered general practitioner, the assessing physician must rule out diseases, defects, or conditions that exclude or condition medical fitness to drive motor vehicles.

(7) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the conditions of medical fitness, the scope of medical examination and specialist examination, the content of the declaration of the applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence, and the diseases, defects, or conditions that exclude or condition medical fitness to drive motor vehicles.

p85.text:
(1) The assessment of medical fitness is carried out by the assessing physician at the request of the applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence. The costs of assessing medical fitness are borne by the applicant.

(2) After carrying out the medical examination, or specialist examination, the assessing physician shall issue a medical fitness report to the applicant.

(3) The medical fitness report must be in written form.

(4) In the medical fitness report, the assessing physician shall state the ascertained health status from the perspective of the medical fitness of the applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence and the assessment of their medical fitness.

(5) The applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence is:

- a) medically fit to drive motor vehicles,

- b) medically fit to drive motor vehicles with a condition,

- c) medically unfit to drive motor vehicles.

(6) In the assessment of medical fitness pursuant to paragraph 5 letter b), the reason and condition of medical fitness shall be stated, and in the assessment of medical fitness pursuant to paragraph 5 letter c), the reason for medical unfitness to drive motor vehicles shall be stated.

(7) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the content and template of the medical fitness report and shall regulate the details of the assessment of medical fitness.

p86.heading: Regular medical examinations
p86.text: If, according to the assessment of medical fitness, the applicant for a driving licence or the holder of a driving licence is medically fit to drive motor vehicles with a condition or medically unfit to drive motor vehicles, the assessing physician shall immediately notify the relevant district authority of this fact and send them a copy of the medical fitness report.

p87.text:
(1) The following persons are obliged to undergo regular medical examinations:

- a) a driver of a vehicle who, in fulfilling tasks related to the performance of special duties, uses a special blue warning light,^2) possibly supplemented by a special acoustic warning signal,

- b) a driver who drives a motor vehicle in an employment relationship and for whom driving a motor vehicle is a type of work agreed upon in the employment contract,

- c) a driver for whom driving a motor vehicle is the subject of self-employment carried out according to a special legal regulation,^28)

- d) a holder of a driving licence of groups C, C+E, D, D+E or subgroups C1, C1+E, D1 and D1+E, who drives a motor vehicle classified in the relevant group or subgroup of the driving licence,

- e) a holder of a certificate for driving instructors for training in driving motor vehicles according to a special legal regulation.^4)

(2) A person specified in paragraph 1 is obliged to undergo an initial medical examination before commencing the activity specified in paragraph 1, and subsequent regular medical examinations every two years until reaching the age of 50, and annually after reaching the age of 50.

(3) A holder of a driving licence who is not a person specified in paragraph 1 is obliged to undergo a regular medical examination no earlier than six months before reaching the age of 60, 65, and 68, and no later than on the day of reaching the specified age, and every two years after reaching the age of 68.

(4) The assessing physician may, based on the result of a regular medical examination, in justified cases, especially taking into account the current health status, determine a shorter term for the next regular medical examination for the person specified in paragraph 1 or 3 (hereinafter referred to as the “obliged person”) than the periods specified in paragraph 2 or 3.

(5) If it is necessary to ascertain the medical fitness of the obliged person, the assessing physician may order a specialist examination.

(6) The costs of a regular medical examination for persons specified in paragraph 1 who are in an employment relationship shall be borne by the employer. For persons specified in paragraph 1 who are not in an employment relationship, and for persons specified in paragraph 3, the costs of a regular medical examination shall be borne by these persons.

(7) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the scope of the regular medical examination.

p88.text:
(1) After conducting a regular medical examination, the assessing physician shall issue a medical fitness report to the obliged person. The medical fitness report must be in written form.

(2) The obliged person is

- a) medically fit to drive motor vehicles,

- b) medically fit to drive motor vehicles with a condition,

- c) medically unfit to drive motor vehicles.

(3) The assessment of medical fitness according to paragraph 4 letter b) shall state the condition of medical fitness.

(4) If, according to the result of the regular medical examination, the obliged person is medically fit with a condition or medically unfit, the assessing physician shall notify this fact to the district office competent according to the place of permanent residence of the obliged person and, for persons specified in § 87 paragraph 1 who are in an employment relationship, also to the employer.

(5) The obliged person must undergo a regular medical examination no later than the day before the expiry date of the medical fitness report.

(6) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the model of the medical fitness report and regulate the details of assessing the medical fitness of the obliged person.

p89.text: An obliged person who does not have a valid medical fitness report is medically unfit to drive motor vehicles.

p90.heading: Professional competence to drive motor vehicles
p90.text:
(1) A driving licence of the relevant group or subgroup may only be granted to a person who has acquired professional competence to drive motor vehicles according to a special legal regulation^4) (hereinafter referred to as “professional competence”).

(2) If an applicant for a driving licence has completed practical training and a driving test for a motor vehicle^4) with a vehicle equipped with an automatic transmission, the district office shall restrict the driving licence of the relevant group or subgroup only to vehicles with this type of transmission; however, this restriction shall not be applied if it concerns a driving licence of group AM or subgroup A1.

(3) When driving rail vehicles,^11) which are used in road traffic, the driver of such vehicle must be a holder of a rail vehicle driving authorisation according to a special legal regulation.^11) A trolleybus driver must be a holder of a group D driving licence.

p91.heading: Further conditions for granting a driving licence
p91.text:
(1) A driving licence

- a) of group D or subgroup D1 may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a group B driving licence,

- b) of group C or subgroup C1 may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a group B driving licence,

- c) of group B + E may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a group B driving licence,

- d) of group C + E may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a group C driving licence,

- e) of subgroup C1 + E may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a subgroup C1 driving licence,

- f) of group D + E may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a group D driving licence,

- g) of subgroup D1 + E may only be granted to an applicant who is already a holder of a subgroup D1 driving licence.

(2) A driving licence granted for group

- a) C + E or D + E is also valid for group B + E,

- b) C + E is also valid for group D + E, if the holder has a group D driving licence.

(3) An applicant for a group A driving licence according to § 83 paragraph 1 letter e) and paragraph 3 must

- a) be a holder of a group A driving licence and pass a supplementary examination according to a special legal regulation,^4) or

- b) fulfil the conditions for granting a group A driving licence and pass a supplementary examination according to a special legal regulation.^4)

p92.heading: Granting and extension of a driving licence
p92.text:
(1) A driving licence shall be granted to an applicant for a driving licence by the competent district authority.

(2) A driving licence shall be granted on the basis of an application for a driving licence, which must be in writing.

(3) The application for a driving licence must state

- a) the first name and surname of the applicant for the driving licence,

- b) the address of permanent residence of the applicant for the driving licence,

- c) the date and place of birth and the birth identification number of the applicant for the driving licence,

- d) the group or subgroup of the driving licence for which the application is made.

(4) The application must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a medical fitness report,

- c) a document of professional competence to drive motor vehicles classified in the relevant group or subgroup of the driving licence,

- d) a document of fulfilment of other conditions pursuant to § 91,

- e) one photograph of dimensions 3.5 x 4.5 cm corresponding to the applicant's appearance at the time of application and showing the applicant in a front-facing view with the height of the facial part of the head from eyes to chin at least 13 mm, in civilian clothes, without dark glasses and without head covering; a photograph with head covering may be submitted only in justified cases, for example for religious or health reasons, which by its execution complies with the requirements of the relevant standard,^29) (hereinafter referred to as „photograph“).

(5) After the application is submitted, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 3 letters a) to c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.

(6) The provisions of paragraphs 1 to 5 shall also apply in the case of extending a driving licence to include another group or subgroup of driving licence.

(7) If the applicant for a driving licence or for an extension of a driving licence fulfils all conditions pursuant to § 82 paragraph 1, they have a legal right to the granting of a driving licence or to its extension to include another group or subgroup of driving licence.

p93.heading: Conditioning and Restriction of Driving Licence
p93.text:
(1) The competent district authority shall condition a driving licence if the holder of the driving licence is medically fit to drive motor vehicles with a condition.

(2) The competent district authority shall restrict a driving licence if the holder of the driving licence

- a) has partially lost professional competence,

- b) has ceased to fulfil any of the other conditions pursuant to § 91,

- c) has waived a certain group or subgroup of the driving licence.

(3) The fact pursuant to paragraph 2 letter c) must be notified in writing by the holder of the driving licence to the competent district authority. The notification must state

- a) the first name and surname of the holder of the driving licence,

- b) the address of permanent residence of the holder of the driving licence,

- c) the date and place of birth and the birth identification number of the holder of the driving licence,

- d) the group or subgroup of the driving licence that the holder of the driving licence is waiving.

(4) The notification pursuant to paragraph 3 must be accompanied by a valid identity document and one photograph of the holder of the driving licence. After the notification is submitted, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 2 letters a) to c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the holder of the driving licence.

(5) In the decision on conditioning the driving licence pursuant to paragraph 1 or on restricting the driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letters a) and b), the competent district authority shall state the extent of the conditioning or restriction of the driving licence and, if applicable, other conditioning or restricting conditions for the exercise of the driving licence that the holder of the driving licence must fulfil. The restriction of the driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter c) shall be acknowledged by the competent district authority on the basis of the notification pursuant to paragraph 3, and it shall not decide on the restriction of the driving licence.

p94.heading: Withdrawal of Driving Licence and Waiver of Driving Licence
p94.text:
(1) The competent district authority shall withdraw a driving licence

- a) if the holder of the driving licence has completely lost medical fitness,

- b) if the holder of the driving licence has completely lost professional competence,

- c) from a person who has been imposed a penalty of prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles by a court or the competent administrative authority.^30)

(2) The holder of a driving licence may waive the driving licence.

(3) The waiver of a driving licence must be notified in writing by the holder of the driving licence to the competent district authority. The notification must state

- a) the first name and surname of the holder of the driving licence,

- b) the address of permanent residence of the holder of the driving licence,

- c) the date and place of birth and the birth identification number of the holder of the driving licence,

- d) the date of granting of the driving licence.

(4) The notification pursuant to paragraph 3 must be accompanied by a valid identity document of the holder of the driving licence. After the notification is submitted, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 3 letters a) to c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the holder of the driving licence.

(5) The waiver of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 shall be acknowledged by the competent district authority on the basis of the notification pursuant to paragraph 3, and it shall not decide on the withdrawal of the driving licence.

p95.heading: Suspension of Driving Licence
p95.text:
(1) Within the proceedings on conditioning, restricting or withdrawing a driving licence, the competent district authority may decide on the suspension of the driving licence as a preliminary measure pursuant to a special legal regulation.^31)

(2) Suspension of a driving licence means that the holder of the driving licence may not drive motor vehicles for the duration of this suspension.

p96.heading: Review of Medical Fitness
p96.text:
(1) The conditioning of health fitness pursuant to § 93 (1) or the loss of health fitness pursuant to § 94 (1) (a) shall be ascertained by a review of health fitness.

(2) The provisions of § 84 to 86 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the review of health fitness.

(3) The competent district authority, within the scope of proceedings concerning the conditioning, restriction or withdrawal of a driving licence, shall order the holder of the driving licence to undergo a review of health fitness pursuant to paragraph 2 within the specified period, if facts emerge that reasonably indicate that the holder of the driving licence is medically fit to drive motor vehicles with a condition or medically unfit to drive motor vehicles.

(4) The holder of the driving licence is obliged to undergo a review of health fitness within the specified period. If the holder of the driving licence is unable to undergo a review of health fitness within the specified period for a serious reason, they shall immediately notify the competent district authority, which shall set an alternative period for them.

(5) If the holder of the driving licence fails to undergo a review of health fitness without a serious reason within the specified period or within the alternative period, they shall be considered medically unfit to drive motor vehicles.

(6) The specified period for the review of health fitness pursuant to paragraph 3 shall not be shorter than 15 days; the alternative period pursuant to paragraph 4 shall not be shorter than 10 days.

p97.heading: Review of professional competence
p97.text:
(1) The loss of professional competence pursuant to § 93 (2) (a) or § 94 (1) (b) shall be ascertained by a review of professional competence.

(2) The review of professional competence shall be carried out pursuant to a special legal regulation.^4)

(3) The competent district authority, within the scope of proceedings concerning the restriction or withdrawal of a driving licence, shall order the holder of the driving licence to undergo a review of professional competence pursuant to paragraph 2 within the specified period, if facts emerge that reasonably indicate that the holder of the driving licence has partially or completely lost professional competence.

(4) The holder of the driving licence is obliged to undergo a review of professional competence within the specified period. If the holder of the driving licence is unable to undergo a review of professional competence within the specified period for a serious reason, they shall immediately notify the competent district authority, which shall set an alternative period for them.

(5) If the holder of the driving licence fails to undergo a review of professional competence without a serious reason within the specified period or fails to undergo a review of professional competence within the alternative period, they shall be considered professionally unfit to drive motor vehicles.

(6) The specified period for the review of professional competence pursuant to paragraph 3 shall not be shorter than 30 days; the alternative period pursuant to paragraph 4 shall not be shorter than 15 days.

Cancellation of conditioning or restriction of a driving licence

p98.text:
(1) If the reasons for the conditioning of a driving licence pursuant to § 93 (1) have ceased to exist, the competent district authority shall decide on the cancellation of the conditioning of the driving licence on the basis of an application.

(2) An application for the cancellation of the conditioning of a driving licence shall be submitted by the holder of the driving licence, whose driving licence has been conditioned, to the competent district authority.

(3) An application pursuant to paragraph 2 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the applicant's permanent residence address,

- c) the applicant's date and place of birth and personal identification number,

- d) the date on which the decision on the conditioning of the driving licence became final,

- e) the conditioning of the driving licence, the cancellation of which is requested.

(4) The application pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a medical fitness report of the applicant issued by an assessing physician, which must not be older than 30 days on the date of submission of the application.

(5) After the submission of the application, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 3 (a) to (c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.

(6) For determining whether the reasons for the conditioning of a driving licence pursuant to § 93 (1) have ceased to exist, the provisions of §§ 84 to 86 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

p99.heading: Return of a driving licence
p99.text:
(1) If the reasons for the restriction of a driving licence due to partial loss of professional competence pursuant to § 93 (2) (b) have ceased to exist, the competent district authority shall decide on the cancellation of the restriction of the driving licence on the basis of an application.

(2) An application for the cancellation of the restriction of a driving licence shall be submitted by the holder of the driving licence, whose driving licence has been restricted due to partial loss of professional competence, to the competent district authority. The application may be submitted no earlier than six months after the date on which the decision on the restriction of the driving licence due to partial loss of professional competence became enforceable or when the decision on the suspension of the driving licence became enforceable.

(3) An application pursuant to paragraph 2 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the applicant's permanent residence address,

- c) the applicant's date and place of birth and personal identification number,

- d) the date of enforceability of the decision on the restriction of the driving licence due to partial loss of professional competence,

- e) the driving licence of the relevant group or sub-group, the return of which is requested.

(4) The application pursuant to paragraphs 2 and 3 must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a document of professional competence to drive motor vehicles classified in the group or sub-group of the driving licence pursuant to paragraph 3 (e), which must not be older than 30 days on the date of submission of the application.

(5) After the submission of the application, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 3 (a) to (c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.

(6) The cessation of reasons for the restriction of a driving licence due to partial loss of professional competence shall be ascertained by re-examination of professional competence pursuant to a special legal regulation.^4)

p100.text:
(1) If the reasons for the withdrawal of a driving licence due to loss of health fitness under § 94 (1) (a) have ceased to exist in whole or in part, the competent district authority shall decide on the application for the return of the driving licence.

(2) An application for the return of a driving licence shall be submitted by the person whose driving licence was withdrawn due to loss of health fitness, to the competent district authority.

(3) If more than three years have elapsed since the decision on the withdrawal of the driving licence due to loss of health fitness became final, the applicant under paragraph 2 must prove professional competence.

(4) The application under paragraph 2 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the applicant's permanent residence address,

- c) the applicant's date and place of birth and birth identification number,

- d) the date on which the decision on the withdrawal of the driving licence due to loss of health fitness became final,

- e) the driving licence of the relevant group or sub-group for which the return is requested.

(5) The application under paragraphs 2 and 4 must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a health fitness assessment issued by the assessing physician, which must not be older than 30 days on the date of submission of the application,

- c) a document of the applicant's professional competence, which must not be older than 30 days on the date of submission of the application, if the application is submitted within the period specified in paragraph 3.

(6) After the submission of the application, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data under paragraph 4 (a) to (c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.

(7) For the purpose of ascertaining whether the reasons for the withdrawal of a driving licence due to loss of health fitness have ceased to exist in whole or in part, the provisions of § 84 to 86 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(8) Professional competence under paragraph 3 shall be ascertained by an examination of professional competence under a special legal regulation.^4)

(9) If the ascertainment under paragraph 7 or the examination of professional competence under paragraph 8 proves that the applicant for the return of a driving licence is medically fit to drive motor vehicles only with a condition or only partially professionally competent to drive motor vehicles, the competent district authority shall condition or restrict the returned driving licence. The provisions of § 93 (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis to this conditioning or restriction.

p101.text:
(1) If the reasons for the withdrawal of a driving licence due to loss of professional competence under § 94 (1) (b) have ceased to exist in whole or in part, the competent district authority shall decide on the application for the return of the driving licence.

(2) An application for the return of a driving licence shall be submitted by the person whose driving licence was withdrawn due to loss of professional competence, to the competent district authority. The application may be submitted no earlier than six months after the date on which the decision on the withdrawal of the driving licence due to loss of professional competence became enforceable or when the decision on the suspension of the driving licence became enforceable.

(3) The application under paragraph 2 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the applicant's permanent residence address,

- c) the applicant's date and place of birth and birth identification number,

- d) the date of enforceability of the decision on the withdrawal of the driving licence due to loss of professional competence,

- e) the driving licence of the relevant group or sub-group for which the return is requested.

(4) The application under paragraphs 2 and 3 must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a document of professional competence to drive motor vehicles classified in the group or sub-group of the driving licence under paragraph 3 (e), which must not be older than 30 days on the date of submission of the application.

(5) After the submission of the application, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data under paragraph 3 (a) to (c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.

(6) The full or partial cessation of reasons for the withdrawal of a driving licence due to loss of professional competence shall be ascertained by an examination of professional competence under a special legal regulation.^4)

(7) If the examination of professional competence under paragraph 6 proves that the applicant for the return of a driving licence is only partially professionally competent to drive motor vehicles, the competent district authority shall restrict the returned driving licence. The provisions of § 93 (5) shall apply mutatis mutandis to this restriction.

p102.heading: Part 2
p102.text:
(1) If the reasons for the withdrawal of a driving licence under § 94 (1) (c) have ceased to exist, the competent district authority shall decide on its return upon application by the person whose driving licence was withdrawn.

(2) The application for return shall be submitted by the person referred to in paragraph 1 to the competent district authority. The application may be submitted no earlier than after the reasons for the withdrawal of the driving licence under § 94 (1) (c) have ceased to exist.

(3) The application under paragraph 2 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the applicant's permanent residence address,

- c) the applicant's date, place of birth and birth identification number,

- d) the date of enforceability of the decision on the withdrawal of the driving licence,

- e) the relevant group or sub-group of the driving licence for which the return is requested.

(4) The application under paragraphs 2 and 3 must be accompanied by a valid identity document of the applicant. After the submission of the application, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data under paragraph 3 according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant.


Driving Licence

p103.text:
(1) A driving licence is a public document that certifies the granting of a driving entitlement to drive motor vehicles classified in the relevant group or sub-group of the driving entitlement and by which the holder proves their first name, surname, birth identification number and likeness, as well as other data recorded therein according to this Act.

(2) A driving licence must not be left or accepted as collateral and must not be confiscated upon entry into premises or onto land.

p104.heading: Types of Driving Licences
p104.text:
(1) In the Czech Republic, a Czech Republic driving licence (hereinafter referred to as "driving licence") and an international driving permit issued by the Czech Republic (hereinafter referred to as "international driving permit") are issued.

(2) The following entitle a person to drive motor vehicles in the territory of the Czech Republic:

- a) a driving licence,

- b) a driving licence issued by a Member State of the European Communities (hereinafter referred to as "European Communities driving licence"),

- c) a driving licence issued by a foreign state pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Vienna 1968) and the Convention on Road Traffic (Geneva 1949) (hereinafter referred to as "driving licence issued by a foreign state"),

- d) an international driving permit issued by a foreign state pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Vienna 1968) and the Convention on Road Traffic (Geneva 1949) (hereinafter referred to as "international driving permit issued by a foreign state"),

provided they are valid at the time of driving a motor vehicle in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(3) An international driving permit does not entitle a person to drive motor vehicles in the territory of the Czech Republic.

(4) An international driving permit issued by a foreign state pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Vienna 1968) is valid for driving motor vehicles in the territory of the Czech Republic for a period of three years from the date of issue; an international driving permit issued by a foreign state pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Geneva 1949) is valid for driving motor vehicles in the territory of the Czech Republic for a period of one year from the date of issue.

(5) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the specimen of the driving licence, the international driving permit, the European Communities specimen driving licence, and the specimen design of a driving licence issued by a foreign state and an international driving permit issued by a foreign state.

p105.heading: Data recorded in the driving licence
p105.text:
(1) A driving licence shall contain the following data:

- a) the holder's first name and surname,

- b) the holder's date and place of birth,

- c) the holder's birth registration number,

- d) a photograph or other form of likeness of the holder,

- e) the municipality of the holder's permanent residence,

- f) the holder's signature,

- g) the categories and subcategories of driving entitlement, the granting of which the driving licence certifies to the holder, and the date of first granting of the driving entitlement for each category and subcategory of driving entitlement,

- h) the date of issue of the driving licence,

- i) the date of validity of the driving licence,

- j) the name, registered office, and stamp impression of the authority that issued the driving licence,

- k) the series and number of the driving licence,

- l) records of restrictions on driving entitlement pursuant to § 93, § 100 para. 9, and § 101 para. 7.

(2) Upon request of the holder of a driving entitlement, the designation of a higher vocational school graduate (hereinafter referred to as "designation"), an academic title, a professional designation, another university graduate title (hereinafter referred to as "title"), or a scientific degree^32) may be entered into the driving licence. If a citizen has obtained multiple designations, titles, or scientific degrees, only one designation, one title, or one scientific degree specified by the citizen shall be entered upon their proposal.

(3) If obtaining the holder's signature on the driving licence is associated with an insurmountable obstacle, it shall be replaced by a thumbprint.

(4) The driving licence shall contain a space for a data chip, into which machine-readable data on the document name, the code of the Czech Republic as the issuing state, the holder's first name and surname, the number and series of the driving licence, the category and subcategory of driving entitlements, and restrictions on driving entitlement shall be recorded.

(5) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the method of recording data pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2 in the driving licence and details concerning the size and placement of the space for the data chip in the driving licence.

Data recorded in the international driving permit

p106.text:
(1) An international driving permit shall contain the following data:

- a) the holder's first name and surname,

- b) the holder's date and place of birth,

- c) a photograph or other form of likeness of the holder,

- d) the municipality of the holder's permanent residence,

- e) the categories and subcategories of driving entitlement, the possession of which the international driving permit certifies,

- f) the date of issue of the international driving permit,

- g) the date of validity of the international driving permit,

- h) the name, registered office, and stamp impression of the authority that issued the international driving permit,

- i) the series and number of the international driving permit,

- j) the series and number of the driving licence on the basis of which the international driving permit was issued,

- k) records of restrictions on driving entitlement pursuant to § 93, § 100 para. 9, and § 101 para. 7.

(2) Upon request of the holder of a driving entitlement, a designation, title, or scientific degree may be entered into the international driving permit. If a citizen has obtained multiple designations, titles, or scientific degrees, only one designation, one title, or one scientific degree specified by the citizen shall be entered upon their proposal.

(3) The validity of an international driving permit shall be determined in accordance with the period of validity of the driving licence on the basis of which the international driving permit was issued, but for a maximum period of three years from the date of issue, if it is an international driving permit issued pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Vienna 1968), and for a period of one year from the date of issue, if it is an international driving permit issued pursuant to the Convention on Road Traffic (Geneva 1949).

(4) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the method of recording data pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2.

p107.text:
(1) If a holder of an international driving permit issued by a foreign state has been sentenced by a court or a competent administrative authority^30) to a prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles, or if their driving entitlement has been made conditional or restricted pursuant to § 93 or withdrawn pursuant to § 94 by a final decision of the district authority, the district authority shall record this fact in the international driving permit issued by the foreign state; the district authority shall simultaneously notify the authority of the foreign state that issued the international driving permit of this fact.

(2) The district authority competent to make the entry and notification pursuant to paragraph 1 is the district authority that issued the decision on making conditional, restricting, or withdrawing the driving entitlement, or that imposed the prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles, or in whose territorial district the court that imposed the prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles is located.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the method of recording the prohibition of activity consisting of a prohibition on driving motor vehicles and the record of making conditional, restricting, or withdrawing the driving entitlement in an international driving permit issued by a foreign state, and the form and method of notification pursuant to paragraph 1.

p108.heading: Change of data in a driving licence or international driving permit
p108.text:
(1) The holder of a driving licence is obliged to notify the competent district authority of any change in the data recorded in the driving licence within five working days from the date on which the change occurred.

(2) In the event of a change in the data recorded in the driving licence, the competent district authority shall issue a new driving licence to the holder of the driving licence.

(3) The provisions of paragraphs 1 and 2 shall also apply in the event of a change in the data recorded in an international driving permit.

Issuance of a driving licence

p109.text:
(1) A driving licence shall be issued to a person who has been granted a driving entitlement.

(2) A driving licence shall also be issued to the holder of a driving entitlement,

- a) whose driving entitlement has been extended to include another group or subgroup of driving entitlement,

- b) whose driving entitlement has been made conditional or restricted or who has waived a certain group or subgroup of driving entitlement pursuant to § 93,

- c) whose conditional status or restriction of driving entitlement has been cancelled pursuant to § 98 or § 99,

- d) whose driving licence is expiring or whose driving licence is invalid pursuant to § 118,

- e) who requests the exchange of a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a driving licence of the European Communities model for a driving licence,

- f) who requests the issuance of a driving licence due to a change in the data specified in the driving licence pursuant to § 108.

(3) The basis for issuing a driving licence for a person specified in paragraph 1 and for the holder of a driving entitlement pursuant to paragraph 2 letter a) is an application for the granting of a driving entitlement or for the extension of a driving entitlement pursuant to § 92.

(4) The basis for issuing a driving licence for the holder of a driving entitlement pursuant to paragraph 2 letter b) is a decision on making conditional or restricting the driving entitlement pursuant to § 93.

(5) The basis for issuing a driving licence for the holder of a driving entitlement pursuant to paragraph 2 letter c) is a decision on the cancellation of the conditional status or restriction of the driving entitlement pursuant to § 98 and 99.

(6) The basis for issuing a driving licence for the holder of a driving entitlement pursuant to paragraph 2 letters d), e) and f) is an application for the issuance of a driving licence.

(7) An application for the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 6 must be in writing and must contain

- a) the applicant's first name and surname,

- b) the address of the applicant's permanent residence in the territory of the Czech Republic, unless the applicant proves that they are preparing for the exercise of a profession in the territory of the Czech Republic for at least six months,

- c) the applicant's date and place of birth and birth number.

(8) The application for the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraphs 6 and 7 must be accompanied by

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) one photograph pursuant to § 92 paragraph 4 letter e),

- c) the driving licence, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 1 letter d),

- d) the driving licence issued by a foreign state or an international driving permit issued by a foreign state or a driving licence of the European Communities model, for which an exchange is requested, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter e),

- e) the applicant's driving licence, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter f).

(9) After submitting the application pursuant to paragraphs 6, 7 and 8, the competent district authority shall immediately

- a) verify the data pursuant to paragraph 7 letters a) to c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the applicant,

- b) record from the driving licence, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter d), or from the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the driving licence of the European Communities, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter e), or from the driving licence, if it concerns the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 2 letter f), the data necessary for the issuance of the driving licence and return the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the driving licence of the European Communities or the driving licence to the applicant.

(10) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the model of the application for the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to paragraphs 6 and 7.

p110.text:
(1) A driving licence shall be issued to the holder of a driving entitlement by the competent district authority.

(2) If the conditions for issuing a driving licence are met, the competent district authority shall issue a driving licence

- a) to a person who has been granted a driving entitlement, or to a holder of a driving entitlement whose driving entitlement has been extended to include another group or subgroup of driving entitlements, on the day on which the decision on the granting or extension of the driving entitlement was made pursuant to § 92,

- b) to a holder of a driving entitlement whose driving entitlement has been made conditional or restricted, or who has waived a group or subgroup of driving entitlements pursuant to § 93, or whose condition or restriction of driving entitlement has been cancelled pursuant to § 98 or § 99, on the day on which the decision on the condition or restriction of driving entitlement or the decision on the cancellation of the condition or restriction of driving entitlement became final,

- c) to a holder of a driving entitlement who applies for the exchange of a driving licence whose validity is expiring or is invalid, or a driving licence issued by a foreign state or an European Communities driving licence for a driving licence, or who applies for the issuance of a driving licence due to a change in the data specified in the driving licence, within 15 working days from the date of submission of the application for the issuance of a driving licence pursuant to § 109 para. 6, 7 and 8.

(3) A person or a holder of a driving entitlement to whom a driving licence is to be issued pursuant to § 109 para. 1 or 2 is obliged to collect the driving licence in person at the competent district authority. If a driving licence is issued to the holder of a driving entitlement pursuant to § 109 para. 2 letter d), e) or f), the holder of the driving entitlement is obliged to simultaneously surrender the invalid driving licence or the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence or the driving licence containing already invalid data upon collection of the driving licence.

(4) A holder of a driving entitlement may not hold more than one valid driving licence.

(5) Neither the holder of a driving licence nor any other unauthorized person may make any entries, changes, corrections or modifications in the driving licence.

(6) The implementing regulation shall stipulate the method of handling driving licence forms when issuing driving licences.

p111.heading: Issuance of an "International Driving Permit"
p111.text:
(1) An "International Driving Permit" shall be issued to the holder of a driving entitlement upon his application.

(2) With the application for the issuance of an "International Driving Permit", the holder of a driving entitlement must submit

- a) a valid identity document of the applicant,

- b) a valid driving licence of the applicant or a valid driving licence of the applicant issued by a foreign state,

- c) one photograph according to § 92 para. 4 letter e).

(3) The holder of a driving entitlement, if the conditions under paragraph 2 are met, has a legal right to the issuance of an "International Driving Permit" certifying the driving entitlement to drive motor vehicles to the extent of his driving entitlement.

(4) The competent district authority shall issue the "International Driving Permit" to the holder of the driving entitlement.

(5) If the conditions for the issuance of an "International Driving Permit" are met, the competent district authority shall issue the "International Driving Permit" without undue delay after the submission of the application for the issuance of an "International Driving Permit".

(6) The applicant for the issuance of an "International Driving Permit" is obliged to collect the "International Driving Permit" in person at the competent district authority.

(7) The holder of a driving entitlement may not hold more than one valid "International Driving Permit" issued under the Convention on Road Traffic (Vienna 1968) and more than one valid "International Driving Permit" issued under the Convention on Road Traffic (Geneva 1949).

(8) Neither the holder of an "International Driving Permit" nor any other unauthorized person may make any entries, changes, corrections or modifications in the "International Driving Permit".

(9) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the method of handling "International Driving Permit" forms when issuing "International Driving Permits".

p112.heading: Issuance of a duplicate driving licence or "International Driving Permit"
p112.text:
(1) A duplicate driving licence or a duplicate "International Driving Permit" shall be issued for a lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed driving licence or "International Driving Permit".

(2) For the issuance of a duplicate driving licence, § 109 para. 6, 7, 8 letter a) and b) and para. 9 and § 110 para. 1, 3, 4, 5 and 6 shall apply mutatis mutandis. The competent district authority shall issue a duplicate driving licence without undue delay after the submission of the application for the issuance of a duplicate driving licence.

(3) For the issuance of a duplicate "International Driving Permit", § 111 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(4) The applicant must attach the damaged driving licence or damaged "International Driving Permit" to the application for the issuance of a duplicate driving licence or a duplicate "International Driving Permit" for a damaged driving licence or damaged "International Driving Permit".

p113.heading: Surrender of a driving licence and an "International Driving Permit"
p113.text:
(1) A driving licence holder whose driving licence is invalid or whose driving entitlement has been:

- a) made conditional or restricted, or who has waived a driving entitlement group or sub-group pursuant to § 93,

- b) withdrawn, or who has waived a driving entitlement pursuant to § 94,

- c) suspended pursuant to § 95,

- d) had the conditionality or restriction of their driving entitlement cancelled pursuant to § 98 or § 99,

is obliged to surrender their driving licence.

(2) The driving licence holder is obliged to surrender the driving licence pursuant to paragraph 1 to the relevant district authority within five working days from the date on which the decision on the restriction of driving entitlement or the decision on the withdrawal of driving entitlement or the decision on the suspension of driving entitlement or the decision on the cancellation of the restriction of driving entitlement became final.

(3) In the event of waiving a driving entitlement group or sub-group pursuant to § 93 paragraph 2 letter c) or waiving a driving entitlement pursuant to § 94 paragraph 2, the driving licence holder is obliged to surrender the driving licence to the relevant district authority simultaneously with the notification of waiving a driving entitlement group or sub-group pursuant to § 93 paragraph 3 or with the notification of waiving a driving entitlement pursuant to § 94 paragraph 3.

(4) If the driving entitlement holder has died, the survivors or the person handling the deceased's estate are obliged to surrender the deceased's driving licence without undue delay to the district authority competent according to the place of the deceased's last permanent residence.
