(5) Upon surrender of the driving licence, the relevant district authority shall issue a document confirming the surrender of the driving licence. The document must state:

- a) the first name and surname of the driving licence holder,

- b) the permanent residence address of the driving licence holder,

- c) the date and place of birth and the birth identification number of the driving licence holder,

- d) the series, number and date of issue of the surrendered driving licence,

- e) the date of surrender of the driving licence.

(6) When surrendering a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 1 letters a) and d), the driving licence holder shall simultaneously present their valid identity document and one photograph, so that the relevant district authority can issue them a driving licence pursuant to § 109 paragraph 2 letters b) and c) and § 110 paragraph 2 letter b).

(7) In the case specified in paragraph 6, the relevant district authority shall immediately verify the data pursuant to paragraph 5 letters a) to c) against the identity document and return the identity document to the driving licence holder.

(8) Paragraphs 1 to 7 also apply to the surrender of an international driving permit.

(9) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the procedure of the district authority for the surrender of a driving licence and an international driving permit, the handling of surrendered driving licences and international driving permits, and shall establish the model of the document confirming the surrender of a driving licence and an international driving permit.

p114.heading: Return of a driving licence and an international driving permit
p114.text:
(1) If a decision has been made to return a driving entitlement pursuant to § 100, 101 or § 102, or if the suspension of a driving entitlement pursuant to § 95 has been cancelled or has ceased to be effective, the relevant district authority shall return the driving licence surrendered pursuant to § 113 paragraph 1 letters b) and c) to the driving entitlement holder without undue delay after the decision on the return of the driving entitlement or the cancellation of the suspension of the driving entitlement has become final, or after the suspension of the driving entitlement has ceased to be effective.

(2) If the relevant district authority, when returning a driving entitlement, has decided to make the driving entitlement conditional or restricted pursuant to § 100 paragraph 9 and § 101 paragraph 7, the driving entitlement holder shall present their valid identity document and one photograph to the relevant district authority after the decision on the conditionality or restriction of the driving entitlement has become final, so that the relevant district authority can issue them a driving licence pursuant to § 109 paragraph 2 letter b) and § 110 paragraph 2 letter b).

(3) In the case specified in paragraph 2, the relevant district authority shall immediately verify the data regarding the first name and surname, permanent residence address, date and place of birth, and birth identification number of the driving entitlement holder against the identity document and return the identity document to the driving entitlement holder.

(4) Paragraphs 1 to 3 also apply to the return of an international driving permit.

p115.heading: Loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence and an international driving permit
p115.text:
(1) The holder of a driving licence shall immediately report the loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence to the competent district authority.

(2) Concurrently with the report under paragraph 1, the holder of a driving licence shall submit a valid identity document to the competent district authority.

(3) The competent district authority shall issue to the holder of a driving licence, immediately after the report under paragraph 1, a confirmation of the notification of the loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence. The confirmation of the notification of the loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence must include:

- a) the first name and surname of the driving licence holder,

- b) the address of permanent residence of the driving licence holder,

- c) the date and place of birth and the birth identification number of the driving licence holder,

- d) the categories and subcategories of the driving licence and the date of their first issue,

- e) the date of loss, theft, damage or destruction of the driving licence,

- f) the date of issue of the confirmation of the notification of the loss, theft, damage or destruction of the driving licence.

(4) In the case specified in paragraph 3, the competent district authority shall immediately verify the data specified in paragraph 3 letters a) to c) according to the identity document and return the identity document to the driving licence holder.

(5) The confirmation of the notification of the loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence is valid for 30 days from the date of its issue; during this period, it replaces the driving licence for the driving licence holder.

(6) After the expiry of the validity period of the notification of loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence, the competent district authority shall issue a duplicate driving licence to the driving licence holder in accordance with § 112.

(7) Paragraphs 1 to 6 shall also apply to the loss, theft, damage or destruction of an international driving permit.

(8) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the template for the confirmation of the notification of loss, theft, damage or destruction of a driving licence or an international driving permit.

p116.heading: Exchange of a driving licence issued by a foreign state, an international driving permit issued by a foreign state, or a European Communities driving licence
p116.text:
(1) A foreign national who is a holder of a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence and has been granted long-term or permanent residence^33) is obliged to apply to the competent district authority for the exchange of the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence for a driving licence within 3 months from the date of granting long-term or permanent residence.

(2) A person who is a holder of a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence, has permanent residence in the territory of the Czech Republic and stays in this territory for more than 185 calendar days per year, is obliged to apply to the competent district authority for the exchange of the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence for a driving licence.

(3) For an application for the exchange of a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2, § 109 paragraphs 7, 8 and 9 and § 110 paragraph 2 letter c), paragraphs 3, 4 and 5 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

(4) The competent district authority shall exchange a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence for a driving licence if the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence is valid. A driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence, in which it is recorded that its holder has been imposed a penalty of prohibition from driving motor vehicles, is considered invalid for the purpose of exchange for a driving licence.

(5) The competent district authority that exchanged a driving licence pursuant to paragraph 1 or 2 shall return the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence to the state that issued this driving licence.

(6) The implementing legal regulation shall specify the details of the procedure for returning a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence pursuant to paragraph 5.

p117.text: Applications and notifications pursuant to § 92 to 94, § 98 to 102, § 108 to 112, § 114 to 116 may be submitted by the applicant or the holder of a driving licence also by post or in electronic form. In such a case, the signature of the applicant or the holder of a driving licence on the application or notification must be officially verified or verified according to a special legal regulation.^34) The applicant or the holder of a driving licence shall not attach an identity document.

p118.heading: Invalidity of driving licences
p118.text:
(1) A driving licence or an international driving permit is invalid if

- a) its validity period has expired,

- b) the driving licence data stated therein do not correspond to reality,

- c) entries, changes, corrections or modifications have been made therein without authorization,

- d) it is damaged in such a way that the records stated therein are illegible.

(2) A driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence, the holder of which is a person registered for long-term or permanent residence in the Czech Republic, is invalid if its holder has not applied for the exchange of the driving licence issued by a foreign state or the European Communities driving licence for a driving licence pursuant to § 116 paragraph 1 or paragraph 2.

p119.text:
(1) The "Driver Register" is a record of data relating to road traffic participants.

(2) The Driver Register contains:

- a) personal data about the driver as stated in the driving licence and in the international driving permit,

- b) records of issued driving licences,

- c) records of granted driving licence categories and subcategories,

- d) records of issued international driving permits,

- e) records of driving licences issued in exchange for a driving licence issued by a foreign state or a European Communities driving licence,

- f) records of surrendered driving licences and international driving permits,

- g) records of committed offences against road traffic safety for which a fine exceeding CZK 2,000 was imposed, for a period of three years from their commission,

- h) data on the revocation of driving entitlements due to loss of health or professional competence to drive motor vehicles and their reinstatement,

- i) data on prohibitions of activity consisting of a ban on driving motor vehicles imposed by a court for committed criminal offences or by an administrative authority for committed offences,

- j) records of driving licences issued in place of lost, destroyed or unusable licences

p120.text:
(1) The "Driver Register" is maintained by the district authority.

(2) If the holder of a driving licence changes their permanent residence, they are obliged to report the change to the district authority at the place of the new permanent residence within 5 working days. In connection with this change, they shall submit their driving licence. The district authority at the place of the new permanent residence shall carry out registration acts and record the changes in the driving licence or issue a new driving licence.

(3) Records of committed offences against road traffic safety, records of the revocation of driving entitlements due to loss of health or professional competence, and records of prohibitions of activity consisting of a ban on driving motor vehicles imposed by a court or an administrative authority shall be entered into the Driver Register only after the relevant decisions have become final. The relevant decisions referred to in the previous sentence shall be sent by the authorities that issued them to the district authority competent according to the place of permanent residence of the citizen.

p121.heading: Issuance of data from the Driver Register
p121.text:
(1) The competent district authority is authorised to provide data from the Driver Register, based on a written request, only to:

- a) state administration bodies to the extent necessary for the performance of their tasks,

- b) courts of all levels, public prosecutor's offices of all levels,

- c) bodies and organisations that are authorised by special regulations to work with such data, for example, an insurance company to ascertain data about a driver who was involved in a traffic accident or caused such an accident,

- d) bodies active in criminal proceedings,

- e) municipalities to the extent necessary for their activities,

- f) natural persons, if it concerns data kept about them,

- g) other natural or legal persons based on the written consent of the person whose data the natural or legal person requests, verified by the competent authority.

(2) The extract of data from the Driver Register shall be governed, unless otherwise provided by this Act, by a special legal regulation.^35)

(3) The Ministry of the Interior, the Police, the Military Police and the Security Information Service must be ensured direct access to the Driver Register.

p122.heading: Central Driver Register
p122.text:
(1) The Ministry maintains the "Central Driver Register". The competent district authority submits updated documents to the central register.

(2) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the method of keeping records in the Driver Register, details of the data kept in the Driver Register, and the method of submitting documents to the Central Driver Register.

p123.heading: Traffic Accident Register and Central Traffic Accident Register
p123.text:
(1) The "Traffic Accident Register" is maintained by the Police.

(2) The Traffic Accident Register contains:

- a) data on the participants in the traffic accident,

- b) data on vehicles involved in the traffic accident,

- c) data on the place and time of the traffic accident,

- d) data on the causes of the traffic accident.

(3) The "Central Traffic Accident Register" is maintained by the Ministry of the Interior. The Police submit updated documents to the Central Traffic Accident Register.

(4) The provisions of § 121 shall apply mutatis mutandis to the issuance of data from the Traffic Accident Register.

(5) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the method of keeping records in the Traffic Accident Register, details of the data kept in the Traffic Accident Register, and the method of submitting documents to the Central Traffic Accident Register.

p124.heading: Competence of ministries, district authorities and the Police
p124.text:
(1) State administration in matters of road traffic is exercised by the Ministry, which is the central body of state administration in matters of road traffic, the district authority, the Ministry of the Interior, and the police.

(2) The Ministry

- a) grants exemptions pursuant to § 43 (5) and § 83 (5),

- b) determines, after a written statement from the Ministry of the Interior, the local and temporary regulation of road traffic on motorways and expressways^1) and the use of operational information devices on motorways and expressways,^1)

- c) approves, upon request and at the expense of the manufacturer or exclusive importer, the design and use of traffic signs, light and acoustic signals, traffic devices, and operational information devices,

- d) decides on appeals against decisions of the district authority issued under this Act,

- e) maintains the central register of drivers,

- f) ensures the production and distribution of driving licenses, international driving permits, and other documents stipulated by this Act,

- g) carries out prevention in the field of road traffic safety.

(3) The district authority

- a) grants exemptions pursuant to § 43 (5),

- b) determines, after a written statement from the relevant district police directorate, the local and temporary regulation of road traffic on roads,^1) excluding expressways, and on local roads^1) and the use of operational information devices on roads,^1) excluding expressways, and on local roads,^1)

- c) authorizes persons entitled to stop vehicles pursuant to § 79 (1) (j),

- d) grants, conditions, restricts, revokes, and reinstates driving entitlements and cancels the conditioning or restriction of driving entitlements,

- e) orders a review of the medical fitness of a driving entitlement holder,

- f) orders a re-examination of the professional competence of a driving entitlement holder,

- g) issues and exchanges driving licenses and international driving permits and issues duplicates of driving licenses and international driving permits,

- h) exchanges driving licenses issued by a foreign state and European Community driving licenses pursuant to § 116,

- i) records facts pursuant to § 107 in an international driving permit issued by a foreign state and notifies the authority of the foreign state that issued this international driving permit,

- j) maintains the register of drivers and issues data from the register of drivers,

- k) issues special vehicle markings pursuant to § 67 to persons who have been granted extraordinary benefits of the second or third degree pursuant to a special legal regulation,^36)

- l) deals with offenses against the safety and smoothness of road traffic pursuant to a special legal regulation,^30)

- m) carries out prevention in the field of road traffic safety.

(4) The Ministry of the Interior maintains the central record of traffic accidents.

(5) The police supervise the safety and smoothness of road traffic by

- a) checking compliance with the obligations of participants and road traffic rules and participating in its management,

- b) clarifying traffic accidents,

- c) maintaining records of traffic accidents,

- d) dealing with offenses against the safety and smoothness of road traffic in block proceedings pursuant to a special legal regulation.^30)

(6) When supervising the safety and smoothness of road traffic, members of the Police in service uniform are authorized, in particular, to

- a) give instructions for managing road traffic,

- b) stop vehicles,

- c) prevent a driver from continuing to drive if continuing to drive would endanger the safety and smoothness of road traffic or if there is reasonable cause to believe that the vehicle has been stolen or is not registered in the prescribed manner or is being used in road traffic with a registration plate other than the assigned one or without a registration plate,

- d) prohibit a driver from driving for a period strictly necessary or order a direction of travel if required by the safety and smoothness of road traffic, or by another public interest,

- e) request a driver to undergo a breath test to determine if they are under the influence of alcohol, and in case of a positive finding, also a medical examination with a blood or urine sample for this determination,

- f) request a driver to undergo a specialized medical examination if they are under the influence of an addictive substance,

- g) request a motor vehicle driver to present documents for driving and operating the vehicle pursuant to § 6 (3),

- h) request a motor vehicle driver to check the maximum permissible axle load, maximum permissible vehicle weight or vehicle combination weight, or the technical condition of the vehicle or vehicle combination,

- i) decide on the removal of a vehicle if it obstructs road traffic,

- j) use technical means to prevent the departure of a vehicle pursuant to a special legal regulation.^37)

p125.heading: Fines
p125.text:
(1) The district authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 100,000 on a natural person carrying out business activities pursuant to a special legal regulation^28) or a legal entity which, as the operator of a vehicle,

- a) ordered or permitted the use of a vehicle in road traffic that does not meet the conditions stipulated by a special legal regulation,^2)

- b) entrusted the driving of a vehicle to a person who did not meet the conditions under § 3 para. 2 and § 5 para. 2 letter b) and c),

- c) entrusted the driving of a motor vehicle to a person who did not meet the conditions under § 3 para. 3,

- d) failed to ensure that the colour scheme and marking of the vehicle were carried out in such a way that they were not interchangeable with the special colour scheme and marking of vehicles of the Military Police^12) and the Police^13) and the Customs Administration.^14)

(2) A fine may be imposed within one year from the day on which the district authority learned of the unlawful act under paragraph 1, but no later than two years from the day on which this act occurred or when the unlawful act was still ongoing. A fine cannot be imposed if a period longer than three years has elapsed since the unlawful act.

(3) The district authority shall also collect the fine. The regulations on the administration of taxes and fees^38) apply to the enforcement of fines.

(4) When determining the fine, the seriousness, manner, duration, and consequences of the unlawful act shall be taken into account.

(5) Fines imposed and collected under this Act are revenue of the state budget.

p126.text:
(1) Pursuant to this Act, driving licences are also issued to persons who perform tasks under special legal regulations.^39)

(2) For persons who are members of diplomatic missions of foreign states or foreign services accredited in the Czech Republic, the Ministry performs the function of the relevant district authority.

p127.text:
(1) Employees of the Ministry, district authorities, and other persons who ensure the production and completion of driving licences are obliged to maintain confidentiality about facts of which they became aware during the implementation of this Act or in direct connection therewith. The obligation of confidentiality continues even after the termination of their employment or service relationship.

(2) The persons specified in paragraph 1 may be released from the obligation to maintain confidentiality

- a) only by the citizen to whom the facts that are the subject of the obligation to maintain confidentiality relate, or

- b) by a senior employee, in the public interest and in written form, stating the scope and purpose of the release from the obligation to maintain confidentiality.

(3) The provisions of paragraphs 1 and 2 do not affect the obligation of the persons specified in paragraph 1 to report certain facts to the authorities competent under special legal regulations.

p128.text:
(1) Courts of all levels are obliged to send a copy or transcript of a final judgment imposing a ban on activity consisting of a ban on driving motor vehicles to the relevant district authority.

(2) Administrative authorities that issued a final decision imposing a ban on activity consisting of a ban on driving motor vehicles are obliged to send a copy or transcript of such decision to the relevant district authority.

(3) Police or Military Police authorities are obliged to send a notification of the imposition of a fine in summary proceedings for an offence against the safety and smoothness of road traffic to the relevant district authority within five working days after the offence has been dealt with.

p129.heading: Relationship to the Administrative Procedure Code
p129.text:
(1) Decisions under § 92, 93, 94, 95, 98, 99, 100, 101, 102, and 125 are governed by the Administrative Procedure Code,^31) unless this Act provides otherwise.

(2) If an applicant for the granting of a driving entitlement or for the extension of a driving entitlement under § 92 is fully satisfied, a driving licence with the granted or extended driving entitlement shall be issued to the applicant instead of a decision.

(3) An appeal against a decision on the withdrawal of a driving entitlement or on the restriction of a driving entitlement shall not have suspensive effect.

p130.heading: Labour-law relations
p130.text: The rights and obligations arising from the labour-law relations of employees to the employer pursuant to a special legal regulation,^40) who, on the effective date of the Act, primarily perform activities concerning the determination of local and temporary traffic regulations on roads, driving entitlements and driving licences, records listed in the driver's register under § 119, and offences against road traffic safety,^30) shall be transferred on the effective date of the Act from the Ministry of the Interior and from the Police to the Ministry and to the district authorities.

p131.heading: Handover of agenda to transport authorities
p131.text: Units of the Ministry of the Interior and the Police, which on the effective date of the Act perform administrative activities concerning the determination of local and temporary traffic regulations on roads, driving licences and driving entitlements, records listed in the driver's register under § 119, and the handling of offences against road traffic safety,^30) are obliged to hand over the file agenda to the relevant units of the Ministry and the district authorities no later than 30 days from the effective date of the Act.

p132.text:
(1) Proceedings initiated before the effective date of the Act shall be concluded according to the existing legal regulations.

(2) Documents concerning driving entitlements and driving licences issued before the effective date of the Act shall be considered as documents issued under this Act.

p133.heading: Scope of validity of existing driving entitlements

p134.heading: Exchange of existing driving licences
p134.text:
(1) Driving licences issued until 30 June 1964 must be exchanged by their holders within two years from the effective date of this Act.

(2) Driving licences issued from 1 July 1964 until the effective date of this Act must be exchanged by their holders within five years from the effective date of this Act.

(3) Driving licences under paragraphs 1 and 2 shall cease to be valid upon the expiry of the respective period set for their exchange.

p135.heading: Validity of existing international driving permits
p135.text: An international driving permit issued under the existing legal regulations shall be valid for the period of its validity even after the effective date of this Act.

p136.heading: Validity of existing special markings
p136.text: The marking of a vehicle transporting a person with severe health impairment, the marking of a vehicle transporting a person with severe mobility impairment, or the marking of a vehicle driven by a person with hearing impairment, which were issued under the existing legal regulations, shall be valid until 30 June 2001.

p137.heading: Authorisation to issue implementing legal regulations

p138.text:
Act No. 12/1997 Sb., on the Safety and Smoothness of Road Traffic, as amended by Act No. 168/1999 Sb. and Act No. 247/2000 Sb., is hereby amended as follows:

- 1. § 2 is repealed.

- 2. § 3 shall read:


The Directorate of the Traffic Police Service shall exercise the powers of the district traffic inspectorate in relation to foreign diplomatic missions in matters of road vehicle registration and approval of the technical roadworthiness of road vehicles.“.

- 3. § 4 is repealed.

- 4. In § 5 (1), letters a), b), c), d), e), g) and k) are repealed.

The existing letters f), h), i) and j) are designated as letters a), b), c) and d).

- 5. In § 5, paragraphs 2 and 3 are repealed, and at the same time, the designation of paragraph 1 is repealed.

- 6. §§ 6 to 8, including footnotes No. 5), 6), 7), 8), 9) and 10), are repealed.

- 7. §§ 9 to 11 are repealed.

p139.text:
Act No. 111/1994 Sb., on Road Transport, as amended by Act No. 38/1995 Sb., Act No. 304/1997 Sb., Act No. 132/2000 Sb. and Act No. 150/2000 Sb., is hereby amended as follows:

- 1. In § 21, paragraph 5 is repealed.

The existing paragraphs 6 to 8 are designated as paragraphs 5 to 7.

- 2. In § 40b, paragraph 1 is repealed.

The existing paragraphs 2 to 6 are designated as paragraphs 1 to 5.

p140.text: § 22 of Act No. 283/1991 Sb., on the Police of the Czech Republic, is hereby repealed.

p141.heading: Offences against the safety and fluidity of road traffic
p141.text:
Act No. 200/1990 Sb., on offences, as amended by Act No. 337/1992 Sb., Act No. 344/1992 Sb., Act No. 359/1992 Sb., Act No. 67/1993 Sb., Act No. 290/1993 Sb., Act No. 134/1994 Sb., Act No. 82/1995 Sb., Act No. 237/1995 Sb., Act No. 279/1995 Sb., Act No. 289/1995 Sb., Act No. 112/1998 Sb., Act No. 168/1999 Sb., Act No. 360/1999 Sb., Act No. 29/2000 Sb., Act No. 121/2000 Sb., Act No. 132/2000 Sb. and Act No. 151/2000 Sb., is amended as follows:

- 1. § 22, including the heading and footnote 2a), shall read:



(1) An offence is committed by anyone who

- a) as a driver of a motor vehicle, exceeds the maximum permitted speed stipulated by a special act^2a) or by a traffic sign by more than 30 km/h in a municipality or by 50 km/h outside a municipality,

- b) as a driver of a motor vehicle, violates a special act^2a) by incorrect driving in that they

- 1. fail to stop the vehicle at the command of a red “Stop” light signal of a traffic light device or a police officer's “Stop” instruction when directing traffic on roads,

- 2. overtake a vehicle on a pedestrian crossing or immediately before it, or overtake a vehicle in a manner that endangers oncoming drivers or other road users, or overtake if they cannot safely merge in front of the vehicle or vehicles they intend to overtake, or overtake without having a clear view ahead for the distance necessary for a safe overtake,

- 3. when driving from a secondary road marked with the traffic sign “Give Way” or “Stop, Give Way” at an intersection, fail to give way to vehicles travelling on the main road or to vehicles approaching from the right, unless the right of way is indicated by a traffic sign,

- 4. enter a railway crossing in cases where it is prohibited by a special act^2a),

- c) by violating a special act^2a), cause a traffic accident which they are obliged to report immediately to a police officer according to a special act^2a),

- d) commit the act specified under letter c) as a driver of a public transport vehicle,

- e) by an act other than those specified under letters a), b), c) and d), violate a special act.^2a)

(2) For an offence under paragraph 1 letter e), a fine of up to CZK 2,000 may be imposed; for an offence under paragraph 1 letters a) and b), a fine of up to CZK 3,000; for an offence under paragraph 1 letter c), a fine of up to CZK 7,000; for an offence under paragraph 1 letter d), a fine of up to CZK 10,000.

(3) A prohibition of activity for up to six months may be imposed on anyone who commits an offence under paragraph 1 letters a) and b) more than twice within a period of twelve consecutive calendar months.

(4) A prohibition of activity for up to one year may be imposed on anyone who commits an offence under paragraph 1 letter c).

(5) A prohibition of activity for up to two years may be imposed on anyone who commits offences under paragraph 1 letter d).

(6) In summary proceedings, a fine of up to CZK 1,000 may be imposed for an offence under paragraph 1 letter e); a fine of up to CZK 2,000 for an offence under paragraph 1 letters a) and b); and a fine of up to CZK 5,000 for an offence under paragraph 1 letters c) and d).

^2a) Act No. 361/2000 Sb., on road traffic and on amendments to certain acts.”.

- 2. In § 52 letter c), the words “offences committed by violating legal regulations on the safety and fluidity of road traffic,” are repealed.

- 3. In § 54, the words “by violating legal regulations on the safety and fluidity of road traffic, if they have not been dealt with by imposing a fine in summary proceedings, and offences” are repealed.

- 4. In § 86 letter a), after the words “the police also offences against”, the words “the safety and fluidity of road traffic according to § 22, against” are inserted.
